Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
1 Overview 
1.1 Purpose 
This document details the open LiteAPIs of IPC, calling methods, application scenarios, etc. This document provides a 
reference for developers who will develop functions based on these APIs. 
1.2 Intended Audience 
This document is intended for third-party manufacturers and developers engaging in application development based on 
the capabilities provided by the IPC. 
Readers should possess the following skills to fully comprehend the content of this document: 
 Proficient computer programming skills. One or more common programming languages such as Java, C++. 
 Good knowledge of networking. Proficiency in network programing and familiarity with common protocols such as UDP, 
TCP, HTTP, RTSP,RTMP,Websocket etc. 
1.3 Reading Instructions 
In order to quickly understand the API functions provided by the platform and to get started, it is recommended to follow 
the following sequence to read the remaining content of this document: 
 Refer to the Glossary section to gain preliminary knowledge of the terminologies that may appear in the document. 
 Refer to the Preparation section for the integration process. 
 Refer to the Protocol Overview section to grasp the API invocation methods. 
 Refer to the API Overview section to gain a preliminary understanding and insight into the capabilities provided by the 
platform. 
 Read the Programming Guide section to understand the APIs required for the functionalities of each module and the 
related dispatch process. By following these steps, you can quickly locate specific module interfaces based on 
integration requirements and understand the API dispatch process. 
 Read the API List section and search for APIs by category. By reading the descriptions of the APIs, understand their 
specific functions and select the ones that are needed. 
 Complete coding and application development in accordance with API definitions. 
1.4 Glossary 
Terminology 
Meaning 
LiteAPI 
Lightweight open interface based on the Restful style of the HTTP protocol. 
Http Method 
Method of the HTTP request. Commonly used methods include GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE. 
Request Headers 
Header information in the HTTP request. 
HTTP URL 
HTTP request path, referring to the part after the domain name/IP (port number). 
For example, the request address is "http://192.168.1.1:80/LAPI/V1.0/System/Time/NTP", then the URL 
refers to " /LAPI/V1.0/System/Time/NTP "

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
RTSP 
Real Time Streaming Protocol (RTSP) is a network application protocol designed specifically for 
entertainment and communication systems to control streaming media service. This protocol is used to 
establish and control media sessions between terminals. Clients of the media server issue VCR commands, 
such as play, record, and pause, in order to control media streams from the server to the client (video on 
demand) or from the client to the server (voice recording) in real time. 
RTP 
Real time Transport Protocol (RTP) is a network transport protocol, which was published by the Multimedia 
Transport Working Group of the IETF in RFC 1889 in 1996.

2 Preparation 
2.1 Environment 
This document aims to introduce the Lite API connection. For other access methods, please refer to the relevant standard 
documents. 
When users connect to the device using Lite API, the server actively listens, and the device actively registers with the 
server. Once the device successfully registers with the server, the server can send Lite API requests to the device through 
the registered network connection to obtain configuration or request live or recorded media streams. Media stream 
forwarding supports two methods: RTMP or RTP over Websocket. 
  
 
  
Hierarchical levels of messages in the network model. 
 
2.2 Integration Development 
Please see Demo.

3 Protocol Overview 
3.1 Protocol  Description 
3.1.1 Communication Model 
 
This protocol defines the LAPI platform access protocol of Uniview Technology.  
After the device initiatively establishes a WebSocket connection with the platform, the platform and the device can 
perform bidirectional signaling communication based on this WebSocket connection.  
The specific message format is detailed in the following section titled "Message Description". The specific API content for 
messages can be found in the section titled "API List". 
3.1.2 Message Description 
3.1.2.1 WS Message  Message Example 
Request Example     
{ 
"RequestURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/PTZ/Capabilities", 
"Method": "GET", 
"Cseq": 1, 
"Data": {} 
} 
  
Response Example 
{ 
"ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/PTZ/Capabilities", 
"ResponseCode": 0, 
"ResponseString": "OK", 
"Cseq": 1,

3.1.2.2 Therelationship between WS Message and Lite API 
The WS Message is transformed through the Lite API, following the conversion rules described below: 
The Data field of the WS Message request message data is the same as the parameters for the Lite API request. 
The Data field of the WS Message response message data is the same as the Data field in the Lite API return parameters. 
Here is a specific example using the Lite API. 
Names 
Add a preset position information for a specified video input channel on a pan-tilt 
device. 
Interface Description 
V1.0 
Interface version 
/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/PTZ/Presets

3.2 Interface Rules 
Transport Mode 
HTTP/TCP,RTSP/TCP, RTSP/TCP/ 
URL format 
/LAPI/V1.0[/Channels/<ID>]/<service-name>/<resource-name>[/<child-resource-
name>][/<ID>]][?<key>=<value>] 
[ ]: Represents a parameter, which can be a string or an unsigned long integer. 
< >: Represents a video input channel (referred to as video channel for short) of a device. 
<ID>: Represents the video channel ID, which is an unsigned long integer. 
<Service-name>: We categorize services into several types, such as system, media, network, etc. 
<resource-name>: Includes physical resources and virtual resources. 
Submission method 
GET, PUT, POST, DELETE 
Data format 
Both requests and responses are in JSON formats. 
Character encoding 
All uses UTF-8 encoding. 
Security authentication 
HTTP Digest 
3.3 Call Authentication 
See Authentication in the Programming Guide section. 
3.4 Rules of Return Values 
The APIs are called in Json format. 
 The request content format is as follows: 
PUT /LAPI/V1.0/System/Time/NTP HTTP/1.1 
{ 
    "Enabled":1, 
    "AddressType":0, 
    "Address":"24.56.178.140", 
    "Port":123, 
    "SynchronizeInterval":60 
} 
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
 The return format is as follows: 
HTTP/1.1 200 Ok 
{ 
    "Response":{ 
        "ResponseURL":"URL", 
        "ResponseCode":0, 
        "SubResponseCode":101, 
        "ResponseString":"Succeed", 
        "StatusCode":0, 
        "Data":"Null" 
    } 
} 
ResponseURL: URL carried in the HTTP request. When the request uses the GET/PUT/DELETE method, the URL in the 
response is the same as the URL in the request. For a POST request, it is the URL of the newly created object. The client 
can use the URL to directly obtain the new object. 
ResponseCode: Indicates the system processing result. 0 means success. 
ResponseString: Indicates the analysis of system processing results. 
Data: Represents the response to a request (GET), often in JSON format. If the URLs are the same, the data format of the 
PUT request is the same as that of the GET response. The Data item in the following tables lists the data of each API. If the 
server processing fails, the data will be “null”. If the HTTP request uses the PUT/POST method, the data in the response 
will be “null”. 
3.5 Public Return Codes 
3.5.1 HTTP Error Codes 
200 Normal: Request completed. 
201 Normal: Followed by POST command. 
202 Normal: Request has been received and is still being processed. 
203 Normal: Partial info -- Only partial info was returned.  
204 Normal: No response -- Request has been received, but there's no response to be returned. 
  
3xx  Redirect 
301 Moved - The requested data has been moved to a new location permanently. 
302 Found - The requested data is temporarily located at a different URL. 
303 See Other - Please refer to another URL, where the response to the request can be found, and GET should be used to 
retrieve the response. 
304 Unmodified - The document was not modified as expected. 
305 Using Proxy - It is necessary to access the requested resource through the proxy provided in the URL. 
306 Unused - No longer in use; this code is kept for future use. 
  
4xx Client Error 
400 Bad Request - The request contains a syntax problem or cannot be fulfilled. 
401 Unauthorized - The client is not authorized to access the data. 
402 Payment Required - Indicates that the billing system is operational. 
403 Forbidden - Access is forbidden, even with authorization. 
404 Not Found - The server cannot find the requested resource; the document does not exist. 
405 Method Not Allowed - The requested resource does not support the corresponding request method.

406 The client browser does not accept the MIME type of the requested page. 
407 Proxy Authentication Required - The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy. 
410 The requested webpage does not exist. 
415 The media type is not supported - The server denies the request because it doesn't support the format of the request 
entity. 
  
5xx   Server Error 
500 Internal Error - The server cannot fulfill the request due to unexpected circumstances. 
501 Not Executed - The server does not recognize or does not support the corresponding request method. 
502 Erroneous Gateway - The server received an invalid response from an upper level server. 
503 Service Unavailable - The server is unable to process the request due to temporary overload or maintenance. 
3.5.2 Internal Error Codes 
Application errors, which are “ResponseCode”: 
0: Succeed 
1: Common Error 
2: Invalid Arguments 
3: Not Authorized 
4: Not Supported 
5: System Busy

4 API Overview 
  
 
This document provides APIs for the following functions: 
 1. Basic device information, including the model, name, and IP of the device. 
 2. Image parameters and OSD configuration of video channels. 
 3. Parameter configuration of audio channels. 
 4. Operations including PTZ control, preset, patrol, drag-to-zoom, absolute movement, and absolute zoom. 
 5. IO configuration and operations. 
 6. Media operations including video, audio, live view, playback, broadcast, two-way audio. 
 7. Event configuration and subscription. 
 8. Smart functions API: face detection, multi-target detection, people flow counting, perimeter protection. 
 9. Device maintenance: log search, device restart, device upgrade, restoring factory defaults.

5 Programming Guide 
5.1 Security 
5.1.1 Authentication 
he device actively registers with the server, and the server verifies the device's authentication information. If the 
authentication is successful, the device registration is completed. 
The device needs to periodically send heartbeat messages to the server to maintain its registered status. 
The device can also initiate a deregistration request to the server. 
Function 
API URL 
Device Register 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/UpServer/Register 
Device Keepalive 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/UpServer/Keepalive 
Device Unregister 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/UpServer/Unregister 
  
 
                                                                 Device Registration Process 
Process Description: 
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
1.       Supports a distributed design, where the Device Management Center manages the platform controller address 
information for device ownership through the device serial number. The Device Management Center sets the Location 
field in the HTTP Header to return the redirect registration address for the device. 
2.       For the initial registration, the server needs to return a 401 Unauthorized status and send a nonce back to the device. 
The device then registers again, based on the returned nonce. 
3.       For subsequent registrations, the device carries authentication information and a cnonce. The server responds by 
returning a signature resign based on the cnonce, which the device uses for authentication. 
4.       If the Device Management Center and the platform controller are designed to be on the same server, the 302 redirect 
step can be omitted. 
5.       After the device completes registration, it initiates a keep-alive to the platform controller. The keep-alive interval can 
be carried in the keep-alive response by the platform controller. The device side can configure the LAPI platform access 
center address. After a configuration change, the device will proactively initiate a deregistration process to the platform 
controller. 
5.1.2 RSA 
Obtain the RSA public key information being used by the device, including the E field and N field of the public key, for 
verifying certain security-sensitive operations. The device needs to retrieve the RSA public key information after every 
restart. 
Function 
API URL 
Get the current RSA info of the system 
GET /LAPI/V1.1/System/Security/RSA

Get the basic device info, including device name, device type, device model, firmware version, etc.

API URL 
Get the basic device info 
GET /LAPI/V1.0/System/DeviceInfo

Set the system time, Daylight Saving Time (DST), NTP server info, and time sync mode.

API URL 
Get or set the system time. 
GET or PUT /LAPI/V1.0/System/Time

Get or set geographic location info. 
Function 
API URL 
Get or set geographic location info. 
GET or PUT /LAPI/V1.0/System/GPS/BasicInfo

F1：The management server queries the channel information and stream information for the channel. 
F2：The management service sends a request to the device to create a media session, specifying the media server's 
streaming address in the request. 
F3：The device initiates a connection to the media server and sends the media stream. 
F4：The management server sends a request to the device to delete the media session.

F1: The client sends an LAPI message to the device to obtain the stream URL for two-way audio. 
F2: Get the URLs for two-way audio through RTSP's DESCRIBE message. 
F3: Initiate stream setup for two-way audio through RTSP's SETUP message. 
F4: Transport audio steam data.

5.11.15 Event and Alarm subscribe 
The client can send a “create subscription task” request to the device to subscribe to events or alarms from the device. 
When an event or alarm is generated on the device, the device will push the event or alarm message to the client. 
Function 
API URL 
Create subscription task 
POST /LAPI/V1.0/System/Event/Subscription 
Keep-alive subscription task 
PUT /LAPI/V1.0/System/Event/Subscription/<ID> 
Delete subscription task 
DEL /LAPI/V1.0/System/Event/Subscription/<ID> 
Push event or alarm 
POST /LAPI/V1.0/System/Event/Notification/… 
Process description: 
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
 
F1: Subscribers initiate a "create subscription task" request to publishers, specifying the recipient info and the validity 
period of the subscription task in the request. 
F2: If an event or alarm occurs, the publisher reports the event or alarm to the recipient. 
F3: Periodically, within the validity period of the subscription task, the subscriber initiates a "subscription keep-alive task" 
to the publisher to ensure the validity of the subscription task. If the subscription task times out and becomes invalid, and 
the subscriber does not initiate a “subscription keep-alive task" request to the publisher, the publisher will automatically 
destroy the expired subscription task. 
F4: If necessary, the subscriber can initiate a "delete subscription task" request to the publisher to actively delete the 
subscription task. 
5.12 Face Recognition 
Process of using the face recognition function: 
1. Enable face capture and face comparison functions on the camera. 
2. Create a person library.  
3. Input person info. 
4. Enable monitoring. 
5. Search data or subscribe to events.

5.13.3 Restore Factory Defaults 
Restore the device to defaults, including Default and Factory Default.  
Default: Restore factory default settings, except user settings and network settings. 
Factory Default: Restore all default settings, including user settings and network settings. 
Function 
API URL 
Restore device to default settings. 
PUT /LAPI/V1.0/System/FactoryReset

6 API List 
6.1 Device Authentication 
6.1.1 Register 
6.1.1.1 Register Interface 
6.1.1.1.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Registration Interface  
1. First registration without authentication information  
2. Server returns 401 Unauthorized with Nonce  
3. Second registration includes Vendor, DeviceType, DeviceCode, Nonce, Sign, Cnonce, etc., for 
authentication  
4. Server returns 302 or 101 with Cnonce, Resign, etc.  
(302 redirect address is in the response header: Location: Domain Name or IP Address)  
5. Client verifies Resign information  
Registration authentication signature algorithm pseudocode example:  
func check_sign(Sign, Vendor, DeviceType, Devicecode, Algo, Nonce, Secret) bool {  
pstr = Vendor + "/" + DeviceType + "/" + Devicecode + "/" + Algorithm + "/" + Nonce(Cnonce)  
nsign = base64(algohash(pstr, Secret))  
if Nsign != Sign {  
return false  
}  
return true  
}  
Where: Secret is the device access LAPI platform authentication key. Algorithm is empty if not provided. 
algohash is the hash function corresponding to algo, defaulting to HMAC_SHA256. Client signs with 
Nonce, server signs with Cnonce 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/UpServer/Register?Vendor=<Vendor>&DeviceType=<DeviceType>&DeviceCode=<Devi
ceCode>&Algorithm=<Algorithm>&Nonce=<Nonce>&Sign=<Sign>&Cnonce=<Cnonce>&Resign=<Resign>&
SessionID=<SessionID> 
Method 
GET 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.1.1.1.2 URL Description 
Name 
Type 
Required 
Example 
Description 
Vendor 
string 
false 
 
Device manufacturer name, length range [1,64].  
UNIVIEW:UNIVIEW  
When receiving a 401 response from the Server, carry the authentication 
during re-registration. 
DeviceType 
intege
r 
false 
 
Device Type. After receiving a 401 response from the Server, re-register for 
authentication.

When a 401 is received from the Server, include during the subsequent Register 
authentication

string

when re-registering for authentication.

string 
false 
 
WebSocket session ID. When initiated by the Server through 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/UpServer/Connection to request the device to establish a 
new connection, it must be carried. The SessionID is the value from the 
Connection response message.

6.1.1.1.6 Example for Response Parameters 
When the Server determines that the device requires Authentication, it returns 401 along with Nonce 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "Request Interface URL", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Nonce": "ABCDRDFHIKL1123" 
        } 
    } 
} 
When the Server determines the device needs to be redirected for Register, it returns 302 along with the redirect Address 
The message body containing Authentication information is returned when required, otherwise it is not included 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "Request Interface URL", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Cnonce": "ABCDRDFHIKL1123", 
            "Resign": "tdfgfhnczdfhtdrsdgqw123" 
        } 
    } 
} 
Otherwise, after completing the Authentication verification, respond with 101 along with the Server's signature information, and
 the protocol is switched to websocket 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "Request Interface URL", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Cnonce": "ABCDRDFHIKL1123", 
            "Resign": "tdfgfhnczdfhtdrsdgqw123" 
        } 
    } 
}

6.2.1.3.5 Response Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Response 
object 
body 
true 
 
+ResponseURL 
string 
body 
true 
the URL in the HTTP request message 
+ResponseCode 
intege
r 
body 
true 
System processing result. 0 indicates success. 
+SubResponseCode 
intege
r 
body 
true 
System processes the sub-results. 0 indicates success. 
+ResponseString 
string 
body 
true 
Analysis of the system processing results 
+StatusCode 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Status Code 
+Data 
object 
body 
false 
Response Result 
++Mode 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Time Sync Mode  
0: Time Sync Mode is set to Default and follows the original Policy  
1: Time Sync Source is from SDK (UI Configuration)  
2: Time Sync Source is from STOR (Photo Server)  
3: Time Sync Source is from NET (NTP Server)  
4: Time Sync Source is from CC (Management Server non-ONVIF 
protocol)  
5: Time Sync Source is from ONVIF (Management Server ONVIF 
protocol)  
6: Time Sync Source is from Cloud Server  
7: Time Sync Source is from GPS/BD module 
6.2.1.3.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/System/Time/SyncMode", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Mode": 0 
        } 
    } 
}

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
NTP Service Enable 
0: Disabled 
1: Enabled 
+++AddressType 
integer 
body 
true 
Address Type 
Currently not supported: IPv6 
0: IPv4 
1: IPv6 
2: Domain Name 
+++IPAddress 
string 
body 
false 
NTP Server IP Address 
When AddressType is 0, this field is required. 
String length range: [0, 63] 
+++DomainName 
string 
body 
false 
NTP Server Domain Name 
When AddressType is 2, this node is mandatory 
String length range: [0, 63] 
+++Port 
integer<uint32> 
body 
false 
NTP Port Value range: [1, 65535] 
+++SynchronizeInterval 
integer 
body 
true 
NTP synchronization time interval  
The following time periods must be converted into time 
values in seconds as units  
Supports 30-86400s 
6.2.1.5.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/System/Time/NTP", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Num": 1, 
            "NTPServerInfos": [ 
                { 
                    "Enabled": 1, 
                    "AddressType": 0, 
                    "IPAddress": "192.168.1.13", 
                    "DomainName": "string", 
                    "Port": 8088, 
                    "SynchronizeInterval": 300 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

6.2.2 log 
6.2.2.1 Query Log 
6.2.2.1.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Query Log 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/Logs 
Method 
POST 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.2.2.1.2 URL Description 
None 
6.2.2.1.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of query conditions 
Value range: [1, 4] 
QueryInfos 
object[] 
body 
false 
List of query condition information  
When the Num value is 0, this node is optional. 
+QryType 
integer 
body 
true 
Query Conditions 
4: Time, UTC format, unit in seconds 
5: Business Type (equivalent to main type) 
6: Operation Type (equivalent to sub-type) 
+QryCondition 
integer 
body 
true 
Query Condition Logic Type 
0: Equal to 
1: More than 
2: Less than 
3: Greater than or equal to 
4: Less than or equal to 
+QryData 
string 
body 
false 
Alarm Type is represented as a string, supporting multi-type queries with 
Alarm Types separated by commas; up to 16 Alarm Type queries are 
supported in a single request; this field is not required when QryCondition 
is 6 or 7; String Length Range: [0, 63]

2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Limit 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of queries per request 
Value range: [1, 100] 
Offset 
integer 
body 
true 
Query starting from the current No.  
The initial No. starts from 0.  
The minimum value of this field is 0. 
6.2.2.1.4 Example for Request Parameters 
{ 
    "Num": 2, 
    "Limit": 100, 
    "Offset": 0, 
    "QueryInfos": [ 
        { 
            "QryType": 4, 
            "QryCondition": 3, 
            "QryData": "1741104000" 
        }, 
        { 
            "QryType": 4, 
            "QryCondition": 4, 
            "QryData": "1741190399" 
        } 
    ] 
}

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
see 8.2 Alarm Type 
+++ID 
integer 
body 
false 
When the log involves Channel ID, this field must be included. Here, 0 
indicates the device itself, while values starting from 1 represent 
specific channel numbers. When related to input/output alarm 
configuration and alarms, it indicates the input/output alarm number. 
Some models do not support this. 
+++LoginName 
string 
body 
true 
Operation User 
String Length Range: [0, 64] 
+++IP 
string 
body 
true 
User Operation IP 
String Length Range: [0, 64] 
+++Result 
integer 
body 
false 
Result: Some models are not supported. 
6.2.2.1.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/System/Logs", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Total": 1, 
            "Offset": 0, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Logs": [ 
                { 
                    "Time": 1685389939, 
                    "MainType": "Alarm", 
                    "SubType": "MotionDetectionStart", 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "LoginName": "admin", 
                    "IP": "192.168.1.2", 
                    "Result": 0 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

object[] 
body 
false 
List of Video Channel Details

+++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Channel ID  
Value Range: [1, ]

string 
body 
true 
Channel Name 
String Length Range: [0, 63]

integer 
body 
true 
Channel Status 
0: Offline 
1: Online 
+++PoEStatus 
integer 
body 
false 
PoE Port Status 
When IsPoEPort=0, this field does not exist 
1: Normal 
2: Power insufficient 
4: Port load power exceeds rated power 
8: Port does not require power supply 
+++StreamNums 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Media Streams  
Supported number of media streams. This field is not present when 
Status=2. 
+++AddressInfo 
object 
body 
false 
Device Address Information  
When Status=2, this field is not present  
Some models do not support this feature. 
++++AddressType 
integer 
body 
true 
Address Type, supports IPv4 and IPv6  
Value range: [0, 1]  
0: IPv4  
1: IPv6 
++++Address 
string 
body 
true 
Device IP Address, Range: [0, 128] 
++++Port 
integer 
body 
true 
Server Port  
Value Range: [1, 65535]  
Default Value: 80 
++++AccessAddress 
string 
body 
true 
Device Access Address 
String Length Range: [0, 63] 
++++MAC 
string 
body 
true 
Access Device MAC Address  
String Length Range: [0, 63]

++AudioFile 
object 
body 
true 
Audio File Import Capability 
+++MaxNum 
integer<uint32> 
body 
true 
Maximum number of alarm audio files 
supported 
including importable and built-in audio files 
+++MaxSize 
integer<uint32> 
body 
true 
Support for importing the maximum size of a 
single audio file (unit: KB) 
+++FormatNum 
integer<uint32> 
body 
true 
Supports the number of audio file formats that 
can be imported 
+++FormatList 
string 
body 
false 
The file supports importing a list of audio 
formats.  
Format: xx;xx;xx;…, this field is optional when 
FormatNum is 0. Currently supports only pcm 
and g711a formats (case-insensitive).  
String length range: [0, 127] 
+++SupportRename 
integer 
body 
false 
Supports the capability to rename alarm audio.  
Supports alarm audio renaming; if the device 
supports it, it is included.  
Some device models are not supported.  
0: Not supported  
1: Supported 
+++SupportMaunalPlay 
integer 
body 
false 
Supports manual play of alarm audio  
If the device supports it, it will be included  
Some device models do not support it  
0: Not supported  
1: Supported 
+++SupportMaunalPlay2 
integer 
body 
false 
Supports manual playback of warning audio 
with parameters and the capability to clear the 
warning audio playback status.  
Parameters are carried if the device supports 
it.  
Some device models do not support this 
feature.  
0: Not Supported  
1: Supported 
++IsSupportedAudioAlarm 
integer 
body 
false 
Support Alarm Sound capability  
Used to determine the capabilities of 
ActID24/25, IPC retained for compatibility with 
older versions of NVR/APP  
0: Not supported  
1: Supported

string

true

string 
body 
false

The PUT method only sets manual data 
Retains eight decimal places 
Some models are not supported

6.2.9 Diagnosis Info 
6.2.9.1 Get System Diagnosis Info 
6.2.9.1.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Obtain the URL for system diagnosis info. The returned URL is a download interface in file stream format.  
IPC has no historical diagnosis info. By default, the latest diagnosis info is exported.  
Some models do not support this interface, need to use the interface 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/Diagnosis/PackStatus to export diagnosis info. 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/Diagnosis/FileURL 
Method 
GET 
How to submit 
data 
application/json

body

body

body

integer 
body 
true

100

++MaxPTRange 
object 
body 
false 
 
+++Up 
integer 
body 
false 
PTZ Vertical Up Max. Angle *100  
Elevation Angle; for example: if the PTZ Vertical Up Max. 
Angle in the interface is set to 15.36, then this value 
should be 1536;  
Default value: 1536 
+++Down 
integer 
body 
false 
PTZ Flip Vertical Downward Max. Size *100  
Tilt Angle; for example: if the PTZ Flip Vertical Downward 
Max. Size is set to 50.00 in the interface, this value 
should be 5000;  
Default Value: 5000 
+++Left 
integer 
body 
false 
PTZ Flip Horizontal Leftward Max. Size *100  
Satisfy Left + Right is less than or equal to 36000  
Default value: 5500 
+++Right 
integer 
body 
false 
PTZ horizontal rightward max. angle * 100

6.3.2 PTZ Control 
6.3.2.1 PTZ Control Command 
6.3.2.1.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Operate the PTZ for a specified video channel 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/PTZ/PTZCtrl 
Method 
PUT 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.3.2.1.2 URL Description 
None 
6.3.2.1.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
PTZCmd 
intege
r 
body 
true 
PTZ Operation Command Code, input in decimal, e.g. "1794 (0x0702)"  
0x0102: Iris Decrease  
0x0104: Iris Increase  
0x0202: Focus Near  
0x0204: Focus Far  
0x0302: Zoom In  
0x0304: Zoom Out  
0x0402: Up  
0x0404: Downward  
0x0502: Rightward  
0x0504: Leftward  
0x0601: Preset Setting Command  
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
0x0602: Preset Call Command  
0x0702: Upper Left  
0x0704: Lower Left  
0x0802: Upper Right  
0x0804: Lower Right  
0x0901: Stop  
0x0A01: Enable Wiper  
0x0A02: Disable Wiper  
0x0B01: Illumination On  
0x0B02: Illumination Off  
0x0C01: Heater On  
0x0C02: Heater Off  
0x0D01: IR On  
0x0D02: IR Off  
0x0D03: Illumination Control  
0x1001: PTZ Patrol with Preset  
0x1002: Disable PTZ Patrol with Preset  
0x1301: Enable Snow Removal Mode  
0x1302: Disable Snow Removal Mode  
0x1403: Enable Scene Lock  
0x1404: Disable Scene Lock 
Para1 
intege
r 
body 
false 
PTZ Command Parameter 1 
When PTZCmd parameter is 0x402–0x804, it indicates horizontal speed. Horizontal 
speed is 0 during "Move Up (0x402)" and "Move Down (0x404)" operations. 
When PTZCmd parameter is "Preset Setting Command (0x0601)" or "Preset Call 
Command (0x0602)", it indicates Preset ID. 
When PTZCmd parameter is "Snow Removal Mode (0x1301)", it indicates 
intensity. 
Other: Not required 
Value range: [1, 9] 
Para2 
intege
r 
body 
false 
PTZ Command Parameter 2  
When the PTZCmd parameter is in the range 0x402–0x804, it represents the 
vertical speed type. The vertical speed is 0 when "Move Left (0x504)" or "Move 
Right (0x502)" is executed.  
This parameter may be omitted when PTZCmd is not in the range 0x402–0x804.  
Value range: [1, 9] 
Para3 
intege
r 
body 
false 
PTZ CMD Parameter 3 is not in use, fill with 0. 
6.3.2.1.4 Example for Request Parameters 
{ 
    "PTZCmd": 1794, 
    "Para1": 2, 
    "Para2": 2, 
    "Para3": 0 
}

body 
true

data

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
None 
6.3.4.1.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
CenterPointX 
integer<ushort
> 
body 
true 
X-coordinate of the drag box center  
Coordinates relative to the width of the play window (Width), with a 
value not exceeding Width  
Default value: 500 
CenterPointY 
integer<ushort
> 
body 
true 
The Y-coordinate of the center point of the selected area. This is the 
coordinate value relative to the height of the Play window (Height), 
which does not exceed the Height value. Default value: 500 
LengthX 
integer<ushort
> 
body 
true 
The drag box width relative to the play window Width should not 
exceed Width; 0 can be filled when center point is located. 
LengthY 
integer<ushort
> 
body 
true 
The height of the drag box relative to the play window's height 
(Height) should not exceed Height; when center point positioning is 
performed, 0 can be entered; 
Width 
integer<ushort
> 
body 
true 
Actual play window width 
Default value: 1920 
Height 
integer<ushort
> 
body 
true 
Actual Play Window Height 
Default Value: 1080 
6.3.4.1.4 Example for Request Parameters 
{ 
    "CenterPointX": 500, 
    "CenterPointY": 500, 
    "LengthX": 0, 
    "LengthY": 0, 
    "Width": 1920, 
    "Height": 1080 
}

6.3.5.3 Add new patrol route information 
6.3.5.3.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Add the preset patrol route information for the specified video channel 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/PTZ/Patrols 
Method 
POST 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.3.5.3.2 URL Description 
None 
6.3.5.3.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of recorded routes, no more than 16 
PatrolInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Recorded Route Configuration 
+ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Patrol Route No.  
Starts from 0. When setting, use the queried No.  
Value range: [0, ] 
+Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of PTZ operation actions 
+Name 
string 
body 
true 
Patrol Route Name 
String Length Range: [1, 64] 
Default Value: Patrol1 
+Actions 
object 
body 
true 
PTZ Operation Action Information 
++Type 
integer 
body 
true 
Patrol Action Type  
Enter in decimal, e.g.: 0x0602 Goto Preset, enter 1538  
Default value: 1538  
0x0102: Decrease Iris  
0x0104: Increase Iris  
0x0202: Near Focus  
0x0204: Far Focus  
0x0302: Zoom In  
0x0304: Zoom Out  
0x0402: Up  
0x0404: Downward  
0x0502: Rightward  
0x0504: Leftward  
0x0601: Preset Setting Command  
0x0602: Preset Call Command  
0x0702: Upper Left  
0x0704: Lower Left  
0x0802: Upper Right  
0x0804: Lower Right  
0x0901: Stop  
0x0A01: Enable Wiper  
0x0A02: Disable Wiper  
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
0x0B01: Illumination On  
0x0B02: Illumination Off  
0x0C01: Heating On  
0x0C02: Heating Off  
0x0D01: IR On  
0x0D02: IR Off  
0x0D03: Illumination Control  
0x1001: PTZ Patrol by Preset  
0x1002: PTZ Patrol by Preset Disable  
0x1301: Snow Removal Mode Enable  
0x1302: Snow Removal Mode Disable  
0x1403: Scene Lock Enable  
0x1404: Scene Lock Disable 
++Para1 
integer 
body 
false 
PTZ CMD Parameter 1, Horizontal Speed  
This parameter is only used when PTZCmd is 0x402–0x804. During "Move Up 
(0x402)" and "Move Down (0x404)", the horizontal speed is 0.  
When PTZCmd is not 0x402–0x804, this parameter is optional; the Preset speed 
uses this field.  
Value range: [1, 9] 
++Para2 
integer 
body 
false 
PTZ CMD Parameter 2, Vertical Speed  
Only applicable when PTZCmd parameter is 0x402–0x804. When "Move Left 
(0x504)" or "Move Right (0x502)" is selected, the vertical speed is 0.  
PTZCmd 0x402/0x404 is required; when PTZCmd is 0x702-0x804, Para1's 
parameter is used while Para2's data is invalid.  
Value range: [1, 9] 
++Para3 
integer 
body 
false 
Lens Zoom Ratio * 100  
When Type is 0x302 or 0x304, this value is mandatory. 
++PresetID 
integer 
body 
false 
Preset No. 
When Type is 0x602, this value is required. 
++Duration 
integer 
body 
false 
Action Duration 
Indicates the duration of a specific action. A value of 0 indicates that the action 
is maintained indefinitely. The unit is ms. 
Value Range: [0, 1800000] 
6.3.5.3.4 Example for Request Parameters 
{ 
    "ID": 8, 
    "Name": "Uniview", 
    "Num": 2, 
    "Actions": [ 
        { 
            "Type": 1282, 
            "Para1": 6, 
            "Para2": 0, 
            "Para3": 0, 
            "Duration": 2000, 
            "PresetID": 1 
        }, 
        { 
            "Type": 2305, 
            "Para1": 0, 
            "Para2": 0, 
            "Duration": 3000 
        } 
    ] 
}

body

PTZ CMD Parameter 1, Horizontal Speed  
This parameter is only used when PTZCmd is 0x402–0x804. During "Move Up 
(0x402)" and "Move Down (0x404)", the horizontal speed is 0.  
When PTZCmd is not 0x402–0x804, this parameter is optional; the Preset speed 
uses this field.  
Value range: [1, 9] 
++Para2 
integer 
body 
false 
PTZ CMD Parameter 2, Vertical Speed  
Only applicable when PTZCmd parameter is 0x402–0x804. When "Move Left 
(0x504)" or "Move Right (0x502)" is selected, the vertical speed is 0.  
PTZCmd 0x402/0x404 is required; when PTZCmd is 0x702-0x804, Para1's 
parameter is used while Para2's data is invalid.

integer 
body 
false

integer 
body 
false 
Action Duration 
Indicates the duration of a specific action. A value of 0 indicates that the action 
is maintained indefinitely. The unit is ms. 
Value Range: [0, 1800000]

V1.0

Method 
POST 
How to submit 
data 
application/json

None

Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
DeviceID 
string 
body

Alarm Device ID, Length range: [0, 64] 
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
AddressType 
integer 
body 
true 
IP Address Type 
Currently only supports IPv4 
0: IPv4 
1: IPv6 
2: Domain Name 
3: Both IPv4 and IPv6 are required 
IPAddress 
string 
body 
true 
IP Address Length range: [0, 64], some devices have a value 
range of 0 to 63 
Port 
integer 
body 
true 
Port Range: [1, 65535] 
Duration 
integer 
body 
true 
Subscription Period  
Unit: seconds, range: [30, 3600]  
The duration for which the subscription can be maintained. Once 
this time limit is reached, the subscription will be automatically 
disconnected. 
Type 
integer 
body 
false 
Subscription Type  
Example: Bit14 set corresponds to decimal number 16384  
Described by BIT positions, each BIT corresponds to a 
subscription type, from the least significant bit (Bit0) to the most 
significant bit (Bit31). A BIT value of 1 indicates the subscription 
type is active.  
Bit0: Device Status Type Alarm  
Bit1: Surveillance Business Alarm  
Bit2: General Smart Alarm  
Bit3: Smart Alarm  
Bit4: Face Recognition  
Bit5: Structured Data  
Bit6: License Plate Recognition  
Bit8: Sensing Data  
Bit10: Person Verification  
Bit11: Vehicle Traffic Data  
Bit12: Fire Detection Alarm  
Bit13: Alarm Image Data  
Bit14: People Counting  
Bit16: Heat Map 
ImagePushMode 
integer 
body 
false 
Image Pushing Method 
When this field is not provided, the default is to push via Base64 
data 
Default is 0 
0: Base64 data or binary data 
1: Local URL 
2: Cloud storage URL 
SubscribePersonCondition 
object 
body 
false 
Subscription User Data Criteria 
+LibIDNum 
integer 
body 
false 
The number of subscribed library IDs  
Max. Size: 16  
When Subscription Type is 0, the LibIDNum and LibIDList fields 
can be omitted  
When Subscription Type is 1, the number of subscribed library 
IDs and their values must be specified. When LibIDNum is set to 
0xFFFF, it indicates subscription to all libraries  
For the Personnel Verification Subscription (Type 1024) 
interface, LibIDNum can only be set to 65535, and LibIDList is set 
to an empty list 
+LibIDList 
object[] 
body 
false 
List of subscribed library IDs 
++LibID 
integer 
body 
false 
Subscribed Library ID  
This field is optional when LibIDNum is 0 
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
SubscribeEvent 
object 
body 
false 
Subscribing to events is optional. If the client request includes 
this parameter, the server must return events indicating a 
successful subscription. 
+Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of subscribed objects 
+SubScribeObjList 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Monitoring Targets 
++EventType 
string 
body 
true 
Subscribe to event types, multiple types separated by commas, 
"All" indicates subscribing to all events 
++PictureType 
integer 
body 
false 
Subscribe to Photo Type  
0: Binary image data or Base64 (new and old specification push 
interfaces coexist in parallel)  
1: Local URL  
2: Cloud URL 
++SourceNum 
integer 
body 
false 
Number of event sources. If no list is provided, it defaults to 
subscribing to all event sources. 
++SourceList 
object[] 
body 
false 
If no list is provided, it will subscribe to all event sources by 
default. 
+++IDType 
integer 
body 
true 
Subscription Sub-device Code Type  
1: GB/T 181-2016 Device No.  
2: GB/T 1400 Device No.  
3: Custom Code  
4: Channel ID 
+++ID 
string 
body 
true 
Sub-device No., Length range: [0, 32]  
If ID=0 and SourceType=Device, it indicates subscribing to device 
events. 
+++SourceType 
string 
body 
true 
Event Source Type, Length Range: [0, 32] see 8.2 Alarm Type

    "DeviceID": "1",

    "Duration": 30, 
    "Type": 0,

        "LibIDList": [

                "LibID": 2 
            } 
        ] 
    }, 
    "SubscribeEvent": { 
        "Num": 1, 
        "SubScribeObjList": [ 
            { 
                "EventType": "All", 
                "PictureType": 0, 
                "SourceNum": 0, 
                "SourceList": [ 
                    { 
                        "IDType": 4,

Matches the last segment of the URL in the Parameter Reference, used

r 
body

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
6.4.4.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Reference 
string 
body 
true 
URL for the client to confirm the push of alarm event 
messages 
<SubscribersID>: To distinguish different subscription 
clients when multiple users log in from the same IP and 
port 
Maximum length is 256 
AlarmInfo 
object 
body 
true 
Alarm Push Message 
+AlarmType 
string 
body 
true 
Alarm Type, see 8.2 Alarm Type 
Max. Length is 256 
+AlarmLevel 
integer 
body 
true 
Alarm Level 0: Level 1, 1: Level 2, 2: Level 3. Only used for 
People Counting. 
+TimeStamp 
integer 
body 
true 
Alarm Time 
+AlarmSeq 
integer 
body 
false 
Alarm No. 
+AlarmSrcID 
integer 
body 
false 
Alarm Source ID. Single Channel fixedly reports 0, Multi-
channel starts from 1. 
+AlarmSrcName or 
SourceName 
string 
body 
false 
Alarm Source Name 
String Length Range: [0, 128] 
Parameter Name for part of device models is 
AlarmSrcName 
Part of device models is SourceName 
Content is generally Channel<AlarmSrcID> 
+RelatedID 
string 
body 
false 
Associated ID for alarm events and alarm data, globally 
unique within the same camera.  
Length is 15 characters.  
This field must be included when the alarm has associated 
data. 
+DeviceID 
string 
body 
false 
Alarm Device ID 
When using the GB protocol, enter the GB Register Code 
(length: [1,64]) 
RelatedObjects 
object 
body 
true 
Alarm associated data target information. When an alarm 
has associated target data to be reported, this field must 
be included. 
+ObjectNum 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Targets 
+ObjectList 
object[] 
body 
true 
Target list: The maximum number of targets is 30, and 
some Device Models have a maximum of 40. 
++ObjectType 
integer 
body 
true 
Target Type 
1: Face 
2: Person 
3: Non-Motor Vehicle 
4: Vehicle 
255: Unidentified/Unrecognized Target Type 
++ObjectID 
integer 
body 
true 
Target ID

string 
body 
false

integer 
body 
true

integer 
body

string 
body

body

AreaNum 
integer 
body 
true 
The number of area rules starts from 0. 0 indicates no area rule report.

object[] 
body 
false

When AreaNum is non-zero, it is required.

integer 
body 
true

Starting from 0 
+ObjectNum 
integer 
body 
true 
Area Rule Current Number of People

2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Reference 
string 
body 
false 
A URL used by the client to confirm the push of alarm event messages  
<SubscriberID>: To distinguish different subscribed clients when multiple 
users log in from the same IP and port 
TimeStamp 
integer 
body 
true 
Report Time UTC Time, the number of seconds since 00:00 on January 1, 
1970 (Unix epoch time) 
Seq 
integer 
body 
false 
Alarm No. 
DeviceID 
string 
body 
true 
When connecting via the National Standard protocol, the Device ID 
should be filled with the National Standard Register Code, with a length 
of [1,32]. 
ChannelID 
integer 
body 
false 
Channel ID starts from 0 
LineNum 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Tripwire Rules 
Starting from 0, 0 indicates no Tripwire Rule Report 
LineRuleDataList 
object[] 
body 
false 
Tripwire rule counting data. LineNum is required when non-zero. 
+LineID 
integer 
body 
false 
Tripwire ID number, starting from 0. 
+BeginPassTime 
string 
body 
true 
Detect Start Time  
YYYYMMDDHHMMSS, Time is in 24-hour format, String Length [0,18]  
Default Value: 20190919121212 
+EndPassTime 
string 
body 
true 
Detect Stop Time 
YYYYMMDDHHMMSS, Time in 24-hour format 
Default: 20190919121218 
+ObjectIn 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of People Entered During Configured Time 
+ObjectOut 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of People Exited within the Configured Time Period 
+TotalBeginTime 
string 
body 
false 
Total People Counting Start Time: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS, Time is in 24-
hour format  
String Length [0,18]  
When the counter is reset, the start time is re-recorded  
Default Value: 20190919121212 
+TotalIn 
integer 
body 
false 
Enter Total  
Restore when the count is reset 
+TotalOut 
integer 
body 
false 
Leave Total  
Resets the count when cleared

            "ObjectOut": 0,

string 
body 
true

see 8.2 Alarm Type

string 
body 
false 
Device No. 
RelatedID 
string 
body 
false 
The associated ID between alarm events and alarm data, 
globally unique within the same camera.

When the alarm has associated data, this field must be 
included.

object 
body 
true

object 
body 
false

float 
body 
true

++Latitude 
float 
body 
true 
PTZ Latitude (PTZ up/down tilt angle) allows configuration 
of maximum and minimum values. See 
/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/PTZ/Capabilities. Precise to two 
decimal places. Default is 0. 
+Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Channels

object[] 
body 
false 
Fire Alarm Channel Information  
Fire Point Channel Information List, this node is mandatory 
when the Num value is not 0

integer 
body 
true

integer 
body

integer 
body 
true

object[] 
body 
true

++++X 
integer 
body

body

body

body

Image count

body 
false

integer 
body 
true

integer 
body 
true

see 7.1.14 Image Type

integer 
body 
true

4: TIFF 
++++Width 
integer 
body 
true 
Image Width 
++++Height 
integer 
body 
true 
Image height

integer 
body 
true

UTC Time, the number of seconds since 00:00 on January 1,

++++DataType 
integer 
body 
true 
Report Image Data Type  
0: Base64 encoded image data  
1: Image URL  
2: Cloud storage image  
Note: Some devices are not supported.

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++++Size 
integer 
body 
false 
The length of the image after Base64 encoding  
Unit: Byte, Max. Size: 3M  
Mandatory when the data type of the reported data is 
Base64 encoded data  
Some device models are not supported 
++++Data 
string 
body 
false 
The Base64 encoded data of the image  
Required when the data type of the reported data is Base64 
encoded data  
Some device models are not supported 
++++URL 
string 
body 
false 
Image URL, which can be used to directly retrieve the 
image. Length range: [0, 256]. Required when the reported 
data is an image URL. Some device models are not 
supported. 
++++UploadID 
string 
body 
false 
Object Upload ID  
String Length [1,32], mandatory when the reported data 
type is Cloud Storage Upload Images  
Some Device Models are not supported. 
++++UploadStatus 
integer 
body 
false 
Upload Status  
0: Upload Succeeded  
1: Upload Failed  
2: Uploading  
Mandatory when the reported data type is Cloud Storage 
Upload Images  
Some device models are not supported. 
++++ErrorCode 
integer 
body 
false 
Reasons for Upload Failure  
Carried when the upload status is 1  
1: Internal error  
2: Camera does not support this function  
3: Device does not support this function  
4: Upload URL is invalid or expired  
5: Network error  
Some device models are not supported 
++++TransferTime 
integer 
body 
false 
The transmission time from upload start to upload end for 
the image 
Unit: ms 
Some device models are not supported

object 
body 
true 
Temperature Alarm Data 
+RuleType 
integer 
body 
true 
Rule Type  
Used for temperature alarm within detection rules  
1: Temperature alarm full screen rule  
2: Temperature alarm single rule (i.e., point, line, and 
polygon rules)  
3: Temperature alarm rule, which includes single rule and 
comparison rule (i.e., the difference between two single rule 
temperature alarm types) 
+RuleId 
integer 
body 
false 
Rule ID Required when RuleType is 2 or 3 
+ReferenceRuleId 
integer 
body 
false 
When RuleType is 3, it is required (the Comparison Rule 
involves comparing two single rules, and the second Rule ID, 
which is the Comparison Rule ID, must be indicated).

integer 
body

Temperature Alarm Criteria 
1: Lower than

+ValueType 
integer 
body 
false 
Alarm Value Type 
1: Highest Temperature 
2: Lowest Temperature 
3: Average Temperature (Point Temperature) 
4: Temperature Difference 
5: Temperature Variation Rate 
+AlarmValue 
float 
body 
false 
Alarm Value  
The alarm value generated by the current alarm type 
represents the temperature variation rate when the 
ValueType is 5, and represents the temperature value with a 
precision of two decimal places for other types.  
Temperature Unit: Celsius  
Temperature Variation Rate Unit: Celsius per minute 
+Threshold 
float 
body 
false 
Alarm Threshold  
When ValueType is 5, it represents the Temperature 
Variation Rate Threshold; otherwise, it represents the 
Temperature Threshold, with a precision of two decimal 
places  
Temperature Unit: Celsius  
Temperature Variation Rate Unit: Celsius per Minute

6.4.12 Push Face Detection Event 
6.4.12.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
When the subscription type in the subscription message is Face Recognition, use this interface to trigger 
Face Detection events. 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/Event/Notification/PersonInfo 
Method 
POST 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.4.12.2 URL Description 
None 
6.4.12.3 Request Parameters

2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Reference 
string 
body 
true 
Push URL 
URL used by the client to confirm the push of alarm event 
messages 
<SubscribersID>: To distinguish different subscribed clients 
when multiple users log in from the same IP and port 
DeviceID 
string 
body 
false 
Device No. 
Camera No. or domain code, returns the Device No. issued by 
Event Subscription 
Mandatory to fill in when the Event Subscription interface 
includes the Device No. 
Some devices are not supported 
String Length Range: [0, 32] 
PersonEventInfo 
object 
body 
false 
Personnel Alarm Information 
+ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Notification Record ID  
Value Range: [1, ] 
+Timestamp 
integer 
body 
true 
Notification Report Time UTC format, unit seconds 
+NotificationType 
integer 
body 
true 
Notification Type 
0: Real-time Notification 
1: Historical Notification 
+FaceInfoNum 
integer 
body 
false 
Face Information Count  
Range: [0, 1]. When no face is detected in the collected data, 
the FaceInfo-related fields may be omitted.  
Default value: 1 
+FaceInfoList 
object[] 
body 
false 
Face Information List  
When the collected information has no face, the FaceInfo 
related fields can be omitted. 
++RecordID 
integer 
body 
true 
Face Access Record ID  
Value Range: [1, ] 
++Type 
integer 
body 
false 
Face Access Record Type 
0: Face Recognition 
1: Comparison Succeeded Alarm 
2: Comparison Failed Alarm 
++RelatedID 
string 
body 
false 
The associated ID linking Alarm Events and Alarm Data is 
globally unique within the same Camera, with a Length of 15 
characters. This field must be included when the alarm has 
associated data. Some devices do not support this feature. 
++PassingTime 
integer 
body 
true 
Passing Time 
UTC format, unit: seconds 
++ChannelName 
string 
body 
true 
Snapshot Channel Name, Range: [1, 63] 
++ChannelID 
integer 
body 
true 
Channel ID  
Value range: [1, ] 
++CompareInfo 
object 
body 
true 
Face Recognition Information 
+++Similarity 
integer 
body 
false 
Similarity 
Match Alarm carries this field 
When the Face Access Record Type is Match Succeeded Alarm 
or Match Failed Alarm, this field must be carried. 
+++PersonInfo 
object 
body 
false 
Event Personnel Information

+++++Number 
string 
body 
false

++++Position 
string 
body 
true 
Person Location Information 
Normalized Image Coordinates: 0-10000 
Rectangle Upper Left and Lower Right Points: 
"138,315;282,684" 
"," and ";" are both English half-angle characters 
Range: [0, 32]

++++AppearTime 
string 
body 
false 
Time of occurrence, optional when not detected  
Format: YYYYMMDDHHMMSSmmm  
The first MM represents month, the second MM represents 
minute, and mmm represents milliseconds  
Fixed string length: 18  
Not supported by some devices  
String length range: [18, 18] 
++++DisAppearTime 
string 
body 
false 
Disappearance Time, optional when not detected 
Format: YYYYMMDDHHMMSSMMM 
The first MM represents month, the second MM represents 
minute, the third MMM represents milliseconds 
Fixed string length: 18 
Some devices do not support this 
String length range: [18, 18]

6.4.13.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Push high-altitude object throwing event 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/System/Event/Notification/FallingObjectsDetection 
Method 
POST 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.4.13.2 URL Description 
None 
6.4.13.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Reference 
string 
body 
true 
URL for the client to confirm the push of Alarm Event messages  
<SubscriberID>: To distinguish different subscribed clients when multiple 
users log in from the same IP and port. 
AlarmType 
string 
body 
true 
Alarm Type 
see 8.2 Alarm Type

2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Default: FallingObjectsDetectionAlarm 
FallingObjectsDetectionAlarm: falling objects 
TimeStamp 
integer 
body 
true 
Report data generation time  
UTC Time, the number of seconds since 00:00:00 on January 1, 1970 
Seq 
integer 
body 
false 
Alarm No. 
SourceID 
integer 
body 
false 
Alarm Source ID 
SourceName 
string 
body 
false 
Alarm Source Name 
String Length Range: [1, 63] 
DeviceID 
string 
body 
false 
Device ID. When connecting via the national standard protocol, enter the 
national standard register code, with a length of [1,32]. 
RelatedID 
string 
body 
false 
The associated ID between alarm events and alarm data, globally unique 
within the same camera.  
Length is 15 characters.  
When the alarm has associated data, this field must be included. 
AlarmPicture 
object 
body 
true 
Image Information 
+ImageNum 
integer 
body 
true 
Image count 
+ImageList 
object[] 
body 
false 
Image Data List  
When the ImageNum is not zero, it must be included. 
++Index 
integer 
body 
true

large and small image indexes of each target information in ObjectInfo 
++Type 
integer 
body 
true 
Image Type 
see 7.1.14 Image Type 
++Format 
integer 
body 
true

0: JPEG 
1: BMP 
2: PNG 
3: GIF 
4: TIFF 
++Width 
integer 
body 
true 
Image Width 
++Height 
integer 
body 
true 
Image height

integer 
body 
true

integer 
body 
true

1: Image URL  
2: Cloud storage image

integer 
body 
false 
The length of the image after Base64 encoding

Mandatory when the data type of the reported data is Base64 encoded 
data

string 
body 
false

integer 
body 
false 
Total number of unique FaceID entries in the database. Note: If an 
individual has multiple base images, the total count here refers to the 
sum of all base image quantities. Individuals without base images still 
occupy one member slot.

integer 
body 
true

string 
body 
true

Used to identify which device the repository belongs to  
Marked by MD5 encryption of the IPC Serial No. Libraries under the 
Channel need to

integer 
body

Face ID

integer 
body 
false 
Processing Result Status Code  
0: Succeeded.  
1: Common execution failed.  
1000: Algorithm initialization failed.  
1001: Face detection failed.  
1002: No face in the picture.  
1003: Failed to analyze JPEG photo.  
1004: Image quality score not met.  
1005: Image scaling failed.  
1006: Face detection disabled.  
1007: Imported image too large.  
1008: Imported image too small.  
1009: Imported image resolution exceeds 2304*2304.  
1010: Imported image does not exist.  
1011: Maximum number of face elements reached.  
1012: Smart algorithm model mismatch.  
1013: Invalid ID number for face library member.  
1014: Invalid image format for face library member.  
1015: Channel deployment has reached device capacity limit.  
1016: Face library is in use on another client.  
1017: Face library files are being updated.  
1018: JSON deserialization failed.  
1019: Base64 decoding failed.  
1020: Encoded size of face photo does not match the actual received 
length.  
1100: Fingerprint operation - Person does not exist.  
1101: Fingerprint operation - Fingerprint module not connected.  
1102: Fingerprint operation - Fingerprint memory processing failed.  
1103: Fingerprint operation - Fingerprint features from LAPI are 
empty.  
1104: Fingerprint operation - Internal error.  
1105: Fingerprint operation - Exceeds storage capacity.  
1106: Fingerprint operation - Invalid fingerprint ID. 
+++FingerprintNum 
string 
body 
true

string[] 
body 
true

integer 
body 
true

object[] 
body 
false 
Face Information Results List 
++++FaceID 
integer 
body 
false 
Face ID 
++++ResultCode 
integer 
body 
false 
Processing Result Status Code  
0: Succeeded.  
1: Common execution failed.  
1000: Algorithm initialization failed.  
1001: Face detection failed.  
1002: No face in the picture.  
1003: Failed to analyze JPEG photo.  
1004: Image quality score not met.  
1005: Image scaling failed.  
1006: Face detection disabled.  
1007: Imported image too large.  
1008: Imported image too small.  
1009: Imported image resolution exceeds 2304*2304.  
1010: Imported image does not exist.  
1011: Maximum number of face elements reached.  
1012: Smart algorithm model mismatch.  
1013: Invalid ID number for face library member.  
1014: Invalid image format for face library member.  
1015: Channel deployment has reached device capacity limit.  
1016: Face library is in use on another client.  
1017: Face library files are being updated.  
1018: JSON deserialization failed.  
1019: Base64 decoding failed.  
1020: Encoded size of face photo does not match the actual received 
length.  
1100: Fingerprint operation - Person does not exist.  
1101: Fingerprint operation - Fingerprint module not connected.  
1102: Fingerprint operation - Fingerprint memory processing failed.  
1103: Fingerprint operation - Fingerprint features from LAPI are 
empty.  
1104: Fingerprint operation - Internal error.  
1105: Fingerprint operation - Exceeds storage capacity.  
1106: Fingerprint operation - Invalid fingerprint ID. 
+++FingerprintNum 
string 
body 
true 
Number of fingerprint processing results 
+++FingerprintList 
string[] 
body 
true 
Fingerprint Processing Results List

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Num 
integer 
body 
true 
The maximum number of query conditions is 4. 
QueryInfos 
object[] 
body 
false 
List of query conditions 
When Num is 0, this field is not included 
+QryType 
integer 
body 
true 
Query Conditions  
0: User Name  
19: ID No.  
20: Gender Query  
38: Library ID  
55: Username  
58: ID No. Query  
95: Extra Long Personnel ID 
+QryContion 
integer 
body 
true 
Query Condition Logical Type 0: Equal to 
+QryData 
string 
body 
true 
Right Value of Query Condition  
QryType=20, value is Gender, with specific enumeration values as follows:  
0: Unidentified, 1: Male  
2: Female, 9: Not Specified  
QryType=55, value is Username  
QryType=58, value is ID No. 
Limit 
integer 
body 
true 
The number of records per query, max. 20 entries. 
Offset 
integer 
body 
true 
Start the query from the current No., with the initial No. starting at 0. 
6.5.2.4.4 Example for Request Parameters 
{ 
    "Num": 1, 
    "QueryInfos": [ 
        { 
            "QryType": 19, 
            "QryCondition": 0, 
            "QryContion": 0, 
            "QryData": "zhangsan" 
        } 
    ], 
    "Limit": 10, 
    "Offset": 0 
}

body 
false

++++++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable  
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be included  
(For compatibility, if less than 28, even if included, it will not be 
active)  
Not supported by some devices  
0: Disabled  
1: Enabled 
++++++ActID 
integer 
body 
true

Parameter Table 
++++++ActParam 
object 
body 
true

ActID and ActParam can be found in [Alarm Linkage Action 
Parameter Table]. 
+++WeekPlan 
object 
body 
true 
Face Monitoring Task Plan 
++++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Plan Switch  
Leave blank to enable by default  
Default value: 1  
0: Disabled  
1: Enabled

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Planned Days: Max. 7 (seven days a week) 
++++Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
+++++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Arming Index  
Default: 1  
0: Sunday  
1: Monday  
2: Tuesday  
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+++++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be included  
(For compatibility, if less than 28, even if included, it will not be 
active)  
Not supported by some devices  
0: Disabled  
1: Enabled

integer 
body 
true

object 
body 
true

WeekPlan 
object 
body 
true 
Face Monitoring Task Plan

integer 
body 
false

Leave blank to enable by default

0: Disabled  
1: Enabled

string 
body

string 
body 
true

Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
+++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

6.6.2 Plan 
6.6.2.1 Obtain the recording schedule of the specified video input channel 
6.6.2.1.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Obtain the recording schedule of the specified video input channel 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Storage/Schedule/Record 
Method 
GET 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.6.2.1.2 URL Description 
None 
6.6.2.1.3 Request Parameters 
None 
6.6.2.1.4 Example for Request Parameters 
None 
6.6.2.1.5 Response Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Response 
object 
body 
true 
 
+ResponseURL 
string 
body 
true 
the URL in the HTTP request message 
+CreatedID 
integer 
body 
false 
Use the POST method, the ID of the created array object, enter -1 
when not in use. 
+ResponseCode 
integer 
body 
true 
System processing result. 0 indicates success. 
+SubResponseCode 
integer 
body 
true 
System processes the sub-results. 0 indicates success. 
+ResponseString 
string 
body 
true 
Analysis of the system processing results 
+StatusCode 
integer 
body 
true 
Status Code

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
+StatusString 
string 
body 
true 
Parsing results corresponding to status codes 
+Data 
object 
body 
true 
Response Result 
++Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
Plan Enable 
0: Disable 
1: Enable 
++RecordRule 
object 
body 
true 
Recording Schedule Rule 
+++PreRecordTime 
integer 
body 
true 
Pre-alarm Recording Time  
Unit is seconds, range: 0,5,10,20,30,60  
0: 0s  
5: 5s  
10: 10s  
20: 20s  
30: 30s  
60: 60s 
+++PostRecordTime 
integer 
body 
true 
Post-Alarm Recording Time  
Unit: seconds, Range: 5,10,30,60,120,300,600  
5: 5 seconds  
10: 10 seconds  
30: 30 seconds  
60: 60 seconds  
120: 120 seconds  
300: 300 seconds  
600: 600 seconds 
++WeekPlan 
object 
body 
false 
Schedule Table 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
Whether the Plan is enabled 
0: Not Enabled 
1: Enabled 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Maximum of 7 (seven days in a week) 
+++Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
++++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Index 
0: Sunday 
1: Monday 
2: Tuesday 
3: Wednesday 
4: Thursday 
5: Friday 
6: Saturday 
++++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of daily time slots  
IPC maximum is 4 segments 
++++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
+++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time, format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
+++++End 
string 
body 
true 
End Time, format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11]

                            { 
                                "Begin": "24:00:00", 
                                "End": "24:00:00" 
                            } 
                        ] 
                    } 
                ] 
            } 
        } 
    } 
}

How to submit 
data

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
None 
6.6.2.2.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
Plan Enable 
0: Disable 
1: Enable 
RecordRule 
object 
body 
true 
Recording Schedule Rule 
+PreRecordTime 
integer 
body 
true 
Pre-alarm Recording Time  
Unit is seconds, range: 0,5,10,20,30,60  
0: 0s  
5: 5s  
10: 10s  
20: 20s  
30: 30s  
60: 60s 
+PostRecordTime 
integer 
body 
true 
Post-Alarm Recording Time  
Unit: seconds, Range: 5,10,30,60,120,300,600  
5: 5 seconds  
10: 10 seconds  
30: 30 seconds  
60: 60 seconds  
120: 120 seconds  
300: 300 seconds  
600: 600 seconds 
WeekPlan 
object 
body 
false 
Schedule Table 
+Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
Whether the Plan is enabled 
0: Not Enabled 
1: Enabled 
+Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Maximum of 7 (seven days in a week) 
+Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Index 
0: Sunday 
1: Monday 
2: Tuesday 
3: Wednesday 
4: Thursday 
5: Friday 
6: Saturday 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of daily time slots  
IPC maximum is 4 segments 
++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
+++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time, format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
+++End 
string 
body 
true 
End Time, format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11]

    "Enabled": 1, 
    "RecordRule": {

        "PostRecordTime": 10 
    },

        "Enabled": 1, 
        "Num": 1, 
        "Days": [ 
            { 
                "ID": 1, 
                "Num": 1, 
                "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                    { 
                        "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                        "End": "24:00:00" 
                    } 
                ] 
            } 
        ] 
    } 
}

++ScaleLevel 
integer<uint32> 
body 
false 
Supported frame extraction speed threshold  
Speeds greater than or equal to this threshold 
support frame extraction  
The absence of this field indicates no support for 
frame extraction  
Some devices do not support this field

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
The encoding capacity occupied by each stream is 
calculated as: INT(width + 15)/16 * INT(height + 15)/16 
* Frame Rate. For example, with main stream 
1280*720@30, auxiliary stream 720*576@30, and 
third stream 352*288@30:  
(INT(1280+15)/16 * INT(720+15)/16 * 30) + 
(INT(720+15)/16 * INT(576+15)/16 * 30) + 
(INT(352+15)/16 * INT(288+15)/16 * 30) = 168480  
INT() denotes rounding down.

integer 
body 
true

false

integer 
body 
true

integer 
body 
false

0: Off 
1: On

integer 
body 
true

0: Off 
1: Basic (Basic Smart Encoding Mode) 
2: Advanced (Advanced Smart Encoding Mode)

6.7.3.4 Control Device Video Stream Session 
6.7.3.4.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Control device video stream session 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ChannelID>/Media/Video/Streams/Sessions/<SessionID>/Control 
Method 
PUT 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.7.3.4.2 URL Description 
Name 
Type 
Required 
Example 
Description 
ChannelID  
integer  
true 
1 
 
SessionID  
string  
true 
session100
1 
 
6.7.3.4.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Type 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Play Control Parameter Type 0: Pause 1: Play 
Range 
string 
body 
false 
Duration Range, used for playback control, referring to the RTSP Range header field. 
Scale 
float 
body 
false 
Scale (see RTSP Scale field), used for playback control  
This field is used for frame extraction playback. Positive numbers indicate forward 
playback speed, negative numbers indicate reverse playback speed, and 0 is invalid.  
For example:  
+2.0 indicates 2x forward playback speed  
-2.0 indicates 2x reverse playback speed 
Speed 
float 
body 
false 
Speed Type (see RTSP Speed field), used for playback control  
Positive values indicate forward play speed, negative values indicate reverse play 
speed, and 0 is invalid  
For example:  
+2.0 indicates 2x forward play speed  
-2.0 indicates 2x reverse play speed  
+254.0 indicates full-speed streaming (no intervals between frames, data not 
discarded), used for the download function  
+255.0 indicates frame-by-frame forward play  
-255.0 indicates frame-by-frame reverse play 
6.7.3.4.4 Example for Request Parameters 
{ 
    "Type": 1, 
    "Range": "clock=19700101T0000-1Z-20220825T014740Z", 
    "Scale": 2, 
    "Speed": 2

++SupportedTimeFormatNum 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of supported Time OSD formats 
++SupportedTimeFormatList 
integer[] 
body 
true 
Time OSD Format  
0: HH:mm:ss (Commonly used in China, the United 
States, the United Kingdom, etc.)  
1: hh:mm:ss t.t. (Commonly used in Spanish-speaking 
countries except Spain)  
2: hh:mm:ss tt (Commonly used in English-speaking 
countries except the United Kingdom)  
3: tt hh:mm:ss (Commonly used in Chinese 
(Singapore))  
4: hh:mm:ss (Commonly used in Chinese (Taiwan))  
5: HH:mm:ss.xxx (Time with milliseconds (ms))  
6: hh:mm:ss.xxx tt (Time with milliseconds (ms)) 
++SupportedDateFormatNum 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Supported Date OSD Formats 
++SupportedDateFormatList 
integer[] 
body 
true 
Date OSD Format  
MMMM represents the English abbreviation for the 
month (e.g., June); dddd represents the English 
abbreviation for the weekday  
0: yyyy-MM-dd  
1: MM-dd-yyyy  
2: yyyyMMdd

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
An RGB color is calculated by combining the relative brightness of red, 
green, and blue primary colors, with each primary color ranging from 
0 to 255. The calculation formula is (65536*Red + 256*Green + Blue). 
For example, to send the color #665432, calculate 65536 * 102 (66 in 
decimal) + 256 * 84 (54 in decimal) + 50 (32 in decimal) = 6706226 
(which is the decimal equivalent of #665432). To send RGB(102, 84, 
50), calculate 65536 * 102 + 256 * 84 + 50 = 6706226. 
++DateFormat 
integer 
body 
true 
Date Format 
0: yyyy-MM-dd 
1: MM-dd-yyyy 
2: yyyyMMdd 
3: MMddyyyy 
4: yyyyMMdd Weekday X 
5: MMddyyyy Weekday X 
100: DD/MM/YYY British style 
101: MM/DD/YYY American style 
102: DD MM YYY Example: British style 28 March, 2014 
103: DD MM YYY Example: American style March 28, 2014 
104: Weekday, DD MM YYY Example: British style Thursday, 28 March, 
2014 
105: Weekday, DD MM YYY Example: American style Thursday, March 
28, 2014 
106: YYY/MM/DD 
107: YYY MM DD Example: 2014 March 28 
108: Weekday YYY MM DD Example: Thursday, 2014 March 28 
++TimeFormat 
integer 
body 
true 
Time Format  
0: HH:mm:ss (commonly used in China, the United States, the United 
Kingdom, etc.)  
1: hh:mm:ss t.t. (commonly used in Spanish-speaking countries except 
Spain)  
2: hh:mm:ss tt (commonly used in English-speaking countries except 
the United Kingdom)  
3: tt hh:mm:ss (commonly used in Chinese (Singapore))  
4: Afternoon hh:mm:ss (commonly used in Chinese (Taiwan))  
5: HH:mm:ss.xxx (ms time)  
6: hh:mm:ss.xxx tt (ms time) 
++FontAlignList 
integer[] 
body 
false 
Font alignment within the area, fixed to 8 areas 
0: Left 
1: Right 
++Margin 
integer 
body 
false 
Number of blank characters at the edge  
0: None  
1: Single  
2: Double

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Color 
integer 
body 
true 
Color  
An RGB color is calculated by combining the relative brightness of red, 
green, and blue primary colors, with each primary color ranging from 0 to 
255. The calculation formula is (65536*Red + 256*Green + Blue). For 
example, to send the color #665432, calculate 65536 * 102 (66 in decimal) + 
256 * 84 (54 in decimal) + 50 (32 in decimal) = 6706226 (which is the 
decimal equivalent of #665432). To send RGB(102, 84, 50), calculate 65536 
* 102 + 256 * 84 + 50 = 6706226. 
DateFormat 
integer 
body 
true 
Date Format 
0: yyyy-MM-dd 
1: MM-dd-yyyy 
2: yyyyMMdd 
3: MMddyyyy 
4: yyyyMMdd Weekday X 
5: MMddyyyy Weekday X 
100: DD/MM/YYY British style 
101: MM/DD/YYY American style 
102: DD MM YYY Example: British style 28 March, 2014 
103: DD MM YYY Example: American style March 28, 2014 
104: Weekday, DD MM YYY Example: British style Thursday, 28 March, 2014 
105: Weekday, DD MM YYY Example: American style Thursday, March 28, 
2014 
106: YYY/MM/DD 
107: YYY MM DD Example: 2014 March 28 
108: Weekday YYY MM DD Example: Thursday, 2014 March 28 
TimeFormat 
integer 
body 
true 
Time Format  
0: HH:mm:ss (commonly used in China, the United States, the United 
Kingdom, etc.)  
1: hh:mm:ss t.t. (commonly used in Spanish-speaking countries except 
Spain)  
2: hh:mm:ss tt (commonly used in English-speaking countries except the 
United Kingdom)  
3: tt hh:mm:ss (commonly used in Chinese (Singapore))  
4: Afternoon hh:mm:ss (commonly used in Chinese (Taiwan))  
5: HH:mm:ss.xxx (ms time)  
6: hh:mm:ss.xxx tt (ms time)

+++ActID 
integer 
body 
true 
Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter

Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage  
11: Trigger Snapshot Upload to FTP  
12: Trigger Snapshot Upload to E-mail  
13: Trigger Snapshot Upload to Both E-mail and FTP  
14: Smart Linkage Snapshot Upload  
15: Linkage Face Thumbnail Snapshot Upload  
16: Alarm Report  
19: Panoramic Linkage  
20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking  
28: Alarm Sound Arming  
29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage 
+++ActParam 
object 
body 
false 
Linkage Item Parameters. The Parameter Config structure varies for 
different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details.

}

integer 
body 
false 
Plan Switch  
Leave blank to enable by default
