0: Disabled  
1: Enabled 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Planned Days: Max. 7 (seven days a week) 
++Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
+++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Arming Index  
Default: 1  
0: Sunday  
1: Monday  
2: Tuesday  
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event 
6.8.2.5.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Alarm/TamperDetection/WeekPlan", 
        "CreatedID": -1, 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "StatusString": "Succeed", 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 1, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                            "End": "23:59:59", 
                            "ArmingType": 0 
                        } 
                    ] 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++DiffValue 
intege
r 
body 
false 
Difference  
When Audio Detection is set to Sudden Rise, Sudden Fall, or Sudden 
Change type, it is used. An Alarm is triggered when the Volume 
experiences a Sudden Rise, Sudden Fall, or Sudden Change exceeding 
the Difference.  
Value Range: [0, 400]  
Default Value: 100 
++Threshold 
intege
r 
body 
false 
Threshold 
When Audio Detection is set to Threshold, it triggers an alarm when 
volume exceeds the threshold 
Value range: [0, 400] 
Default value: 50 
++BaseValue 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Ambient Sound Volume Reference Value, used when the Detection 
Type is set to Volume Difference. Default: 50. 
++AlThshold 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Alarm Threshold, used only when the Detection Type is Volume 
Difference. Default: 40

{

        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "StatusString": "Succeed", 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "DetectType": 0, 
            "DiffValue": 100, 
            "Threshold": 100, 
            "BaseValue": 50, 
            "AlThshold": 40 
        } 
    } 
}

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Planned Days: Max. 7 (seven days a week) 
++Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
+++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Arming Index  
Default: 1  
0: Sunday  
1: Monday  
2: Tuesday  
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

        "ResponseString": "Succeed",

        "StatusString": "Succeed",

            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 4, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00",

2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Range: [-100, 1000] 
++++++Compensation 
float 
body 
true 
Temperature Compensation 
Accurate to two decimal places, unit: Celsius 
+++CompareRuleDetectionConfigList 
object 
body 
true 
Comparison Rule Detection Configuration 
++++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Temperature Alarm Comparison Rules 
Counting starts from 0 
Value range: [0, 6] 
++++ConfigList 
object[] 
body 
true 
Comparison Rule Detection Parameters Config 
+++++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Rule No. 
Counting begins at 0. 
Value range: [0, 5] 
+++++Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
Enable  
0: Disable  
1: Enable 
+++++Name 
string 
body 
true 
Rule Name 
String Length Range: [0, 20] 
+++++FirstCompareRuleID 
integer 
body 
true 
The No. of the first comparison rule  
Range: [0, 11]  
Default value: 1 
+++++SecondCompareRuleID 
integer 
body 
true 
The No. of the Second Comparison Rule 
The No. of the First Rule cannot be the same. 
Value range: [0, 11] 
++AlarmConfig 
object 
body 
true 
Alarm Trigger Condition Configuration 
+++SingleRuleAlarmConfigList 
object 
body 
true 
Single Rule Alarm Conditions Configuration 
++++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Temperature Alarm Rule Count 
Range: [0, 12] 
++++ConfigList 
object[] 
body 
true 
Single Rule Detection Parameters Config 
+++++Type 
integer 
body 
true 
Alarm Type  
Note: 1. When it is a Comparison Rule, the Alarm 
Type only supports 0, 1, and 2; 2. When the Area 
Type is a point, the Alarm Type only supports 2 and 4  
0: Highest Temperature  
1: Lowest Temperature  
2: Average Temperature  
3: Temperature Difference  
4: Temperature Variation Rate 
+++++Condition 
integer 
body 
true 
Alarm Condition 
0: Lower than 
1: Higher than 
2: Match 
+++++Threshold 
float 
body 
false 
Threshold Temperature (Unit: Degree, required 
when Alarm Type is not Temperature Variation 
Rate), accurate to one decimal place 
+++++ChangeRate 
float 
body 
false 
Temperature Variation Rate Unit: degree/Minute 
(This item is required when Alarm Type is 
Temperature Variation Rate), rounded to two 
decimal places.

Location 
Required 
Description

object 
body 
true

object 
body 
true

integer 
body 
true

object[] 
body 
true

integer 
body 
true 
Rule No. Starts from 0. Value range: [0, 11]

integer 
body 
true 
Enable  
0: Disable  
1: Enable

string 
body 
true 
Rule Name 
String Length Range: [0, 24]

object 
body 
true 
Temperature Measurement Area Information 
++++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
The number of vertices in the Detection Area shape

Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
1: Point 
2: Line 
3-6: Polygon 
Value range: [1, 6] 
++++PointList 
object 
body 
true 
Coordinates of each vertex of the detection area 
graphic 
+++++X 
integer 
body 
true 
X-axis coordinate 
Unit: permyriad 
Default value: 0 
Value range: [0, 10000] 
+++++Y 
integer 
body 
true 
Y-axis coordinate 
unit permyriad 
Default value: 0 
Value range: [0, 10000] 
+++TemperatureDetectionParam 
object 
body 
true 
Temperature Detection Parameters 
++++Emissivity 
integer 
body 
true 
Emissivity of the object under test 
Range: [1, 100] 
++++Distance 
float 
body 
true 
Temperature Measuring Distance Unit: Meter, 
Accuracy: 0.1 Meter 
++++EnvironmentTemperature 
integer 
body 
true 
Environment Temperature 
Unit: degrees 
Range: [-100, 1000] 
++++Compensation 
float 
body 
true 
Temperature Compensation 
Accurate to two decimal places, unit: Celsius 
+CompareRuleDetectionConfigList 
object 
body 
true 
Comparison Rule Detection Configuration 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Temperature Alarm Comparison Rules 
Counting starts from 0 
Value range: [0, 6] 
++ConfigList 
object[] 
body 
true 
Comparison Rule Detection Parameters Config 
+++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Rule No. 
Counting begins at 0. 
Value range: [0, 5] 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
Enable  
0: Disable  
1: Enable 
+++Name 
string 
body 
true 
Rule Name 
String Length Range: [0, 20] 
+++FirstCompareRuleID 
integer 
body 
true 
The No. of the first comparison rule  
Range: [0, 11]  
Default value: 1 
+++SecondCompareRuleID 
integer 
body 
true 
The No. of the Second Comparison Rule 
The No. of the First Rule cannot be the same. 
Value range: [0, 11] 
AlarmConfig 
object 
body 
true 
Alarm Trigger Condition Configuration 
+SingleRuleAlarmConfigList 
object 
body 
true 
Single Rule Alarm Conditions Configuration

Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Temperature Alarm Rule Count 
Range: [0, 12] 
++ConfigList 
object[] 
body 
true 
Single Rule Detection Parameters Config 
+++Type 
integer 
body 
true 
Alarm Type  
Note: 1. When it is a Comparison Rule, the Alarm Type 
only supports 0, 1, and 2; 2. When the Area Type is a 
point, the Alarm Type only supports 2 and 4  
0: Highest Temperature  
1: Lowest Temperature  
2: Average Temperature  
3: Temperature Difference  
4: Temperature Variation Rate 
+++Condition 
integer 
body 
true 
Alarm Condition 
0: Lower than 
1: Higher than 
2: Match 
+++Threshold 
float 
body 
false 
Threshold Temperature (Unit: Degree, required when 
Alarm Type is not Temperature Variation Rate), 
accurate to one decimal place 
+++ChangeRate 
float 
body 
false 
Temperature Variation Rate Unit: degree/Minute (This 
item is required when Alarm Type is Temperature 
Variation Rate), rounded to two decimal places. 
+++Range 
float 
body 
true 
Temperature permissible error. Unit: degree, accurate 
to two decimal places. 
+++Duration 
integer 
body 
true 
Duration, unit: seconds 
Value range: [1, 100] 
Default value: 1 
+CompareRuleAlarmConfigList 
object 
body 
true

r 
body

Enable

Name 
string 
body 
true 
Rule Name 
String Length Range: [0, 20] 
BasicRule 
object 
body 
true 
Basic Rule Information 
+Type 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Alarm Type  
Note: 1. When it is a Comparison Rule, the Alarm Type only supports 
0, 1, and 2; 2. When the Area Type is a point, the Alarm Type only 
supports 2 and 4  
0: Highest Temperature  
1: Lowest Temperature  
2: Average Temperature  
3: Temperature Difference  
4: Temperature Variation Rate 
+Condition 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Alarm Condition 
0: Lower than 
1: Higher than 
2: Match 
+Threshold 
float 
body 
false 
Threshold Temperature (Unit: Degree, required when Alarm Type is 
not Temperature Variation Rate), accurate to one decimal place 
+ChangeRate 
float 
body 
false 
Temperature Variation Rate Unit: degree/Minute (This item is 
required when Alarm Type is Temperature Variation Rate), rounded 
to two decimal places. 
+Range 
float 
body 
true 
Temperature permissible error. Unit: degree, accurate to two 
decimal places. 
+Duration 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Duration, unit: seconds 
Value range: [1, 100] 
Default value: 1 
FirstCompareRuleID 
intege
r 
body 
true 
The No. of the first comparison rule  
Range: [1, 12]  
Default: 1 
SecondCompareRuleID 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Second Comparison Rule No. 
Value Range: [1, 12] 
Default Value: 2

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event 
6.8.5.23.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Alarm/TemperatureDetection/WeekPlan",

        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 1, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                            "End": "23:59:59", 
                            "ArmingType": 0 
                        } 
                    ] 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
+StatusCode 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Status Code 
+Data 
object 
body 
true 
Response Result 
++Enabled 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Area Enable Identifier 
0: Disable 
1: Enable 
++Sensitivity 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Sensitivity 
Range: [1, 100] 
++Grid 
string 
body 
true 
The detection area is divided into a 22x18 grid covering the entire 
image, numbered sequentially from the upper left corner to the lower 
right corner (from left to right and top to bottom), resulting in 396 
grids. An 8-bit binary number (with a maximum value of 255) is used to 
represent the motion detection area selection status of 8 grids: a bit 
value of 1 indicates the grid is selected, while 0 indicates it is not 
selected (bit positions are set from least significant to most significant 
bit). This converts all grids into an array of 50 numbers each containing 
8 bits. The array is first compressed using the PackBits algorithm and 
then encoded with Base64 to generate the compressed string. String 
length range: [0, 256] 
6.8.6.9.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Alarm/MotionDetection/Areas/Grid", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Sensitivity": 50, 
            "Grid": "DWAAAYAABj/5mP/mAAAY/gAgP/4A//77//vv/++//74ABvgBG+AAb4ABvh/2+H/YAf8A" 
        } 
    } 
}

V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Alarm/MotionDetection/Areas/GridArea 
Method 
PUT 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.8.6.12.2 URL Description 
None 
6.8.6.12.3 Request Parameters 
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52
ubox_wlOXx1  2026/2/10 14:55:52


 
Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Enabled 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Area Enable Identifier 
0: Disable 
1: Enable 
Sensitivity 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Sensitivity 
Range: [1, 100] 
Grid 
string 
body 
true 
The detection area is divided into a 22x18 grid covering the entire image, 
numbered sequentially from the upper left corner to the lower right corner (from 
left to right and top to bottom), resulting in 396 grids. An 8-bit binary number 
(with a maximum value of 255) is used to represent the motion detection area 
selection status of 8 grids: a bit value of 1 indicates the grid is selected, while 0 
indicates it is not selected (bit positions are set from least significant to most 
significant bit). This converts all grids into an array of 50 numbers each 
containing 8 bits. The array is first compressed using the PackBits algorithm and 
then encoded with Base64 to generate the compressed string. String length 
range: [0, 256] 
6.8.6.12.4 Example for Request Parameters 
{ 
    "Enabled": 1, 
    "Sensitivity": 100, 
    "Grid": "DWAAAYAABj/5mP/mAAAY/gAgP/4A//77//vv/++//74ABvgBG+AAb4ABvh/2+H/YAf8A" 
}

intege
r

true

intege
r 
body 
true

string 
body

intege
r

        "ResponseCode": 0,

        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": null

}

integer 
body

Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter 
configuration set in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage

20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking

29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage

object 
body 
false

different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details.

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event 
6.8.6.15.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Alarm/MotionDetection/WeekPlan", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 1, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                            "End": "23:59:59", 
                            "ArmingType": 0 
                        } 
                    ] 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

20: Link to Attribute Collection

body

string

string 
body 
true

++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

One-click Focus (Locked): Performs auto focus

+++FocusSceneNum 
integer<uint32> 
body 
true 
Number of supported Scenes 
+++FocusSceneList 
integer<uint32>[] 
body 
false 
Supported Focus Scenes  
Normal scenes are suitable for focus in 
common scenarios such as roads and parks.  
Long Distance scenes are suitable for focus in 
distant road scenarios, e.g., when the mount 
height is more than 30m.  
Indoor Mode is suitable for focus in indoor 
scenarios.  
When FocusSceneNum is 0, this node is 
optional.  
0: Normal  
1: Long Distance  
2: Indoor Mode

object 
body 
true

4: IR Cover Lamp 
5: Warm Light Mode 
6: White Light and IR Dual Source Mode 
7: Smart White Light Mode 
++++LampCtrlModeListNum 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of supported control modes 
++++LampCtrlModeList 
integer[] 
body 
false 
Supported Control Modes  
Global Mode and Overexposure Restrain 
Mode: Lamp switching is linked with Day/Night 
Mode. The specific illumination intensity is 
calculated by the AE algorithm.  
Preset-Road Mode or Preset-Park Mode: Lamp 
switching is linked with Day/Night Mode. The 
specific illumination intensity is calculated in 
real-time based on the current zoom ratio and 
PTZ angle, combined with the preset table file.  
Manual Mode: Linked with Day/Night Mode. 
The illumination intensity is determined by the 
interface settings.

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Manual-Force On Mode: Not linked with 
Day/Night Mode. The illumination is forcibly 
turned on.  
Night Video Detection Mode: Linked with 
Day/Night Mode and Smart Virtual Iris status. 
The illumination is turned on when the current 
Day/Night Mode is Night and the Smart Virtual 
Iris is enabled; otherwise, it remains off. The 
intensity is determined by the interface 
settings.  
When LampCtrlModeListNum is 0, this node is 
optional.  
0: Global Mode Auto Control  
1: Overexposure Restrain Auto Control  
2: Preset Control - Road Mode  
3: Manual Control Mode  
4: Preset Control - Park Mode  
5: Auto Sensitivity Control Mode  
6: Indoor Auto Mode  
7: Manual-Force On Mode  
8: Night Video Detection Mode  
9: PTZ Control Mode

body

object 
body 
true

body 
true

body

body 
true

++++BottomRight 
object 
body 
true

integer<uint32> 
body 
true

+++++Y 
integer<uint32> 
body 
true 
Lower Right Y-coordinate (Percentage) must not be less 
than the Upper Left Y-coordinate. Value Range: [0, 100]

body 
true

integer 
body 
false

If the Image capability supports this Function, this field is

0: Ultra Low

+Time 
integer 
body 
false

seconds  
If the image capability supports this function, this field is 
mandatory  
Value range: [3, 120] 
LinearAntiFlicker 
integer 
body 
false 
Linear Stripe Suppression Level 
Range: [1, 9] 
The Image capability set LinearAntiFlickerList supports this 
function. This field is mandatory 
Some Device Models do not support this feature

+++SupportMode 
integer 
body 
true 
Supported Panoramic Linkage Modes  
Some device models are not supported  
0: The device does not support Gun and Dome 
Camera Linkage function  
1: The device supports Gun and Dome Camera 
Linkage with Box Camera function  
2: The device supports Gun and Dome Camera 
Linkage with Dome Camera function  
3: The device supports Structured Gun and Dome 
Camera Linkage with Box Camera function, differing 
from mode 1 by whether Mixed-Traffic Detection is 
simultaneously supported  
4: The device supports Structured Gun and Dome 
Camera Linkage with Dome Camera function, differing 
from mode 2 by whether Face Detection is 
simultaneously supported

6.10.2 Elevator Entrance Detection 
6.10.2.1 Obtain the Elevator Entrance Detection configuration information for the specified channel 
6.10.2.1.1 Overview 
Name 
Description 
Description 
Retrieve the elevator entrance detection configuration information for the specified channel (not 
supported by some device models). 
Version 
V1.0 
Address 
/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Smart/AccessElevator/Rule 
Method 
GET 
How to submit 
data 
application/json 
6.10.2.1.2 URL Description

r

6.10.2.1.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Smart/AccessElevator/Rule", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1 
        } 
    } 
}

3: Storage

Arming Configuration Information

body

++FaceQualityAnalysis 
object 
body 
false 
Quality Analysis Configuration Parameters 
+++Enabled 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Face Quality Analysis Enable 
0: Disabled 
1: Enabled 
+++Mode 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Face Quality Analysis Mode  
0: Normal Mode, the quality is analyzed each time the 
target appears  
1: Speed Priority Mode, the quality is analyzed once when 
the target appears  
2: Fast Mode, the number of quality analyses for a target is 
determined by the PreferenceFastNum parameter 
+++PreferenceFastNum 
intege
r 
body 
true 
The number of analyses in Periodic Selection Mode is only 
active in the Periodic Selection Mode. 
++FaceQualityAnalysisEx 
object 
body 
true 
Extended Field for Quality-Preferred Configuration Items 
+++Enabled 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Face Quality Analysis Switch Enable 
0: Disable 
1: Enable 
+++Mode 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Face Quality Analysis Mode, Effect Priority Mode: The 
camera continuously tracks a detected face until it leaves 
the frame, outputting the highest quality snapshot from 
previously detected images. Speed Priority: Based on 
different speed levels, outputs the highest quality image 
within a limited time, reporting each target only once. 
Periodic Selection: At fixed intervals, the algorithm 
automatically selects and sequentially outputs the optimal 
face according to detection order (main application 
scenario is interactive face recognition scenarios).  
0: Effect Priority Mode  
1: Speed Priority Mode  
2: Periodic Selection Mode 
+++PreferenceLastTime 
intege
r 
body 
true 
The duration in Face Selection Fast Mode, unit in seconds. 
When the Face Quality Analysis Mode is set to Speed 
Priority, this parameter is valid. Value range: [1, 1800]. 
+++QualityNum 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Number of Selected Photos. This parameter is valid in 
Effect Priority and Speed Priority modes. Value range: [1, 
3]

r

r

intege
r 
body 
true 
Right roll angle, rotating leftward around the Z-axis of the

intege
r 
body 
true 
Left Yaw Angle, rotated left around the Y-axis of the right-

facing

++++YawAngleRight 
intege
r 
body 
true

++++PitchAngleUp 
intege
r 
body 
true

++++PitchAngleDown 
intege
r 
body 
true

Name

FaceCount 
object 
body 
false 
Face Counting Configuration Item  
Mandatory when the device is supported 
+Enabled 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Enable, Face/People Counting based on Face Detection 0: 
Disabled 1: Enabled 
+Direction 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Counting Direction, Person/Face Counting Direction 
0: Counting Direction is Enter 
1: Counting Direction is Leave 
FaceSnapshot 
object 
body 
true 
Face Recognition Configuration Item 
+Mode 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Face Snapshot Mode 
Default: 1 
1: Smart Trigger 
2: Alarm Input/Output Trigger 
3: Smart Trigger + Alarm Input/Output Trigger 
+Num 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Number of Face Snapshots 
Default Value: 10 
Value Range: [1, 30] 
+MarkFace 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Face overlay box switch. If a large image snapshot is captured, 
the face location will be marked in the large image.  
Default value: 1  
0: Disable Face Overlay Box  
1: Enable Face Overlay Box 
PersonSnapshot 
object 
body 
true 
Human Body Snapshot 
+Enabled 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Enable Human Body Snapshot 
0: Disable 
1: Enable 
FaceQualityAnalysis 
object 
body 
false

object 
body

integer 
body 
true

++++HeightMin 
integer 
body 
true

integer 
body 
true

integer 
body 
true

integer 
body

integer 
body 
true 
Face Max. Size Height, expressed in ten-thousandths. Some Device 
Types are not supported. 
+++PupilDistanceMin 
integer 
body 
true 
Minimum inter-pupillary distance of the face, filtered based on the 
inter-pupillary distance. Targets with an inter-pupillary distance 
less than this minimum configuration will be filtered out. Unit: 
permyriad  
Value range: [1, ]  
Default value: 40 
+++PointNum 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of vertices in the detection area graphic

object[] 
body 
true

permyriad

integer 
body

integer 
body 
true

unit permyriad

Value range: [0, 10000]

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++Actions 
object[] 
body 
false 
Interlocking Action Information Table  
When the number of interlocking actions (Num) is more than 0, this 
field is mandatory. 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable 
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be carried 
0: Not supported 
1: Supported 
+++ActID 
integer 
body 
true 
Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter 
configuration set in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage  
11: Trigger Snapshot Upload to FTP  
12: Trigger Snapshot Upload to E-mail  
13: Trigger Snapshot Upload to Both E-mail and FTP  
14: Smart Linkage Snapshot Upload  
15: Linkage Face Thumbnail Snapshot Upload  
16: Alarm Report  
19: Panoramic Linkage  
20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking  
28: Alarm Sound Arming  
29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage 
+++ActParam 
object 
body 
false 
Linkage Item Parameters. The Parameter Config structure varies for 
different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details. 
6.10.4.9.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Smart/FaceDetection/LinkageActions?ChannelID=<ChannelID>", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Actions": [ 
                { 
                    "Enabled": 1, 
                    "ActID": 0, 
                    "ActParam": {} 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

integer 
body 
false

integer 
body 
true

object[] 
body

integer 
body

2: Tuesday

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event 
6.10.4.11.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Smart/FaceDetection/WeekPlans?ChannelID=<ChannelID>", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 1, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                            "End": "23:59:59", 
                            "ArmingType": 0 
                        } 
                    ] 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

r

6.10.4.12.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Planned Days: Max. 7 (seven days a week) 
Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
+ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Arming Index  
Default: 1  
0: Sunday  
1: Monday  
2: Tuesday  
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
false 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field

++TimingResetEnabled 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Enable Reset Counter at Schedule 
0: Do not reset

++ResetTime 
string 
body 
true 
Reset Counter at Time. Required when Reset Counter is enabled. 
Reset occurs at a specific time (24-hour format) in the day. Format: 
XX:XX:XX. Length is limited to 12. Default value: 00:00:00.

            "TimingResetEnabled": 0, 
            "ResetTime": "00:00:00"

    } 
}

integer 
body 
true 
Status Code 
+Data 
object 
body 
true 
Response Result 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of interlocks: Maximum number is 32, and some devices have 
a maximum number of 13. 
++Actions 
object[] 
body 
false 
Interlocking Action Information Table  
When the number of interlocking actions (Num) is more than 0, this 
field is mandatory. 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable 
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be carried 
0: Not supported 
1: Supported 
+++ActID 
integer 
body 
true 
Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter 
configuration set in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage  
11: Trigger Snapshot Upload to FTP  
12: Trigger Snapshot Upload to E-mail  
13: Trigger Snapshot Upload to Both E-mail and FTP  
14: Smart Linkage Snapshot Upload  
15: Linkage Face Thumbnail Snapshot Upload  
16: Alarm Report  
19: Panoramic Linkage  
20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking  
28: Alarm Sound Arming  
29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage 
+++ActParam 
object 
body 
false 
Linkage Item Parameters. The Parameter Config structure varies for 
different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details. 
6.10.5.5.6 Example for Response Parameters

Name 
Description 
Description 
Configure Linked Actions for Mixed-Traffic Detection 
Version 
V1.0

data 
application/json

None 
6.10.5.6.3 Request Parameters 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of interlocks: Maximum number is 32, and some devices have a 
maximum number of 13. 
Actions 
object[] 
body 
false 
Interlocking Action Information Table  
When the number of interlocking actions (Num) is more than 0, this field is 
mandatory. 
+Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable 
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be carried 
0: Not supported 
1: Supported 
+ActID 
integer 
body 
true 
Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter configuration set 
in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage

9: Linkage Start Local Storage

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Planned Days: Max. 7 (seven days a week) 
++Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
+++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Arming Index  
Default: 1  
0: Sunday  
1: Monday  
2: Tuesday  
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
+++LaneCode 
integer 
body 
true 
Lane ID, Custom 
Value range: [1, 254] 
Default value: 1 
+++LaneDescription 
string 
body 
true 
Lane Description 
Default: Lane1 
+++IsOnlyCapHead 
integer 
body 
true 
Vehicle Head Only? 
0: No 
1: Yes 
+++DetectType 
integer 
body 
true 
Detection Object Type, combined bit-wise, each bit corresponds to a 
target type, a value of 0 indicates not to report, and 1 indicates to 
report  
0x00000000: All not reported  
0x00000001: Pedestrian (1)  
0x00000010: Motorcycle (2)  
0x00000100: Non-Motor Vehicle (4)  
0x00001000: Small Vehicle (8)

                    "Enabled": 0, 
                    "LaneCode": 1, 
                    "LaneDescription": "Lane1", 
                    "IsOnlyCapHead": 0, 
                    "DetectType": 0 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

Vehicle Head Only? 
0: No 
1: Yes 
++DetectType 
intege
r 
body 
true 
Detection Object Type, combined bit-wise, each bit corresponds to a 
target type, a value of 0 indicates not to report, and 1 indicates to 
report  
0x00000000: All not reported  
0x00000001: Pedestrian (1)  
0x00000010: Motorcycle (2)  
0x00000100: Non-Motor Vehicle (4)  
0x00001000: Small Vehicle (8)

r

string 
body 
true 
Reset Counter at Time. Required when Reset Counter is enabled. 
Reset occurs at a specific time (24-hour format) in the day. Format: 
XX:XX:XX. Length is limited to 12. Default value: 00:00:00.

Name

Location 
Required

++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of interlocks: Maximum number is 32, and some devices have

++Actions 
object[] 
body 
false 
Interlocking Action Information Table  
When the number of interlocking actions (Num) is more than 0, this 
field is mandatory. 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable 
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be carried 
0: Not supported 
1: Supported 
+++ActID 
integer 
body 
true 
Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter 
configuration set in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage  
11: Trigger Snapshot Upload to FTP  
12: Trigger Snapshot Upload to E-mail  
13: Trigger Snapshot Upload to Both E-mail and FTP  
14: Smart Linkage Snapshot Upload  
15: Linkage Face Thumbnail Snapshot Upload  
16: Alarm Report  
19: Panoramic Linkage  
20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking  
28: Alarm Sound Arming  
29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage 
+++ActParam 
object 
body 
false 
Linkage Item Parameters. The Parameter Config structure varies for 
different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details.

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event 
6.10.6.15.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Smart/TrafficMonitoring/WeekPlan", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 1, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                            "End": "23:59:59", 
                            "ArmingType": 0 
                        } 
                    ] 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Linkages 
Value Range: [0, 31] 
Some Device Models have a maximum number of 13 
++Actions 
object[] 
body 
false 
Interlocking Action Information Table. This node can be omitted when 
Num is 0. 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable 
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be carried 
0: Not supported 
1: Supported 
+++ActID 
integer 
body 
true 
Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter 
configuration set in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage  
11: Trigger Snapshot Upload to FTP  
12: Trigger Snapshot Upload to E-mail  
13: Trigger Snapshot Upload to Both E-mail and FTP  
14: Smart Linkage Snapshot Upload  
15: Linkage Face Thumbnail Snapshot Upload  
16: Alarm Report  
19: Panoramic Linkage  
20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking  
28: Alarm Sound Arming  
29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage 
+++ActParam 
object 
body 
false 
Linkage Item Parameters. The Parameter Config structure varies for 
different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details.

        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Actions": [ 
                { 
                    "Enabled": 1, 
                    "ActID": 0, 
                    "ActParam": {} 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

Name

Location 
Required 
Description 
ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Detection Area Index  
Starts from 0, currently supports up to 4 areas 
Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
Detection Area Enable 
0: Disable 
1: Enable 
Sensitivity 
integer 
body 
true 
Sensitivity 
Range: [1, 100] 
Direction 
integer 
body 
true 
Direction: The clockwise direction (left side) from the rule line start point 
is A, and the counterclockwise direction (right side) is B.  
0: A < - > B, Direction AB Both  
1: B -> A, Direction B to A  
2: A -> B, Direction A to B 
StartPoint 
object 
body 
true

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of interlocks: Maximum number is 32, and some devices have 
a maximum number of 13. 
++Actions 
object[] 
body 
false 
Interlocking Action Information Table  
When the number of interlocking actions (Num) is more than 0, this 
field is mandatory. 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable 
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be carried 
0: Not supported 
1: Supported 
+++ActID 
integer 
body 
true 
Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter 
configuration set in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage  
11: Trigger Snapshot Upload to FTP  
12: Trigger Snapshot Upload to E-mail  
13: Trigger Snapshot Upload to Both E-mail and FTP  
14: Smart Linkage Snapshot Upload  
15: Linkage Face Thumbnail Snapshot Upload  
16: Alarm Report  
19: Panoramic Linkage  
20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking  
28: Alarm Sound Arming  
29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage 
+++ActParam 
object 
body 
false 
Linkage Item Parameters. The Parameter Config structure varies for 
different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details.

            "Num": 1,

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event 
6.10.9.9.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Smart/CrossLineDetection/WeekPlan", 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 1, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                            "End": "23:59:59", 
                            "ArmingType": 0 
                        } 
                    ] 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
+StatusCode 
integer 
body 
true 
Status Code 
+StatusString 
string 
body 
true 
Parsing results corresponding to status codes 
+Data 
object 
body 
true 
Response Result 
++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Plan Switch  
Leave blank to enable by default  
Default value: 1  
0: Disabled  
1: Enabled 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Planned Days: Max. 7 (seven days a week) 
++Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
+++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Arming Index  
Default: 1  
0: Sunday  
1: Monday  
2: Tuesday  
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0,

2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of interlocks: Maximum number is 32, and some devices have 
a maximum number of 13. 
++Actions 
object[] 
body 
false 
Interlocking Action Information Table  
When the number of interlocking actions (Num) is more than 0, this 
field is mandatory. 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable 
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be carried 
0: Not supported 
1: Supported 
+++ActID 
integer 
body 
true 
Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter 
configuration set in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage  
11: Trigger Snapshot Upload to FTP  
12: Trigger Snapshot Upload to E-mail  
13: Trigger Snapshot Upload to Both E-mail and FTP  
14: Smart Linkage Snapshot Upload  
15: Linkage Face Thumbnail Snapshot Upload  
16: Alarm Report  
19: Panoramic Linkage  
20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking  
28: Alarm Sound Arming  
29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage 
+++ActParam 
object 
body 
false 
Linkage Item Parameters. The Parameter Config structure varies for 
different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details.

Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Smart/LeaveZone/WeekPlan", 
        "CreatedID": -1, 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "StatusString": "Succeed", 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 1, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                            "End": "23:59:59", 
                            "ArmingType": 0 
                        } 
                    ] 
                } 
            ] 
        }

body

integer 
body 
true

Days 
object[] 
body 
true

+ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Arming Index  
Default: 1  
0: Sunday  
1: Monday  
2: Tuesday  
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+Num 
integer 
body 
true

object[] 
body 
true

On the same day, time periods must not overlap.

string 
body 
true

string 
body 
true

integer 
body

Linkage Item  
IDActID is associated with ActParam, with linkage parameter 
configuration set in ActParam.  
Enumeration see 8.2 Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table  
3: Storage  
4: PTZ Preset  
5: Trigger Boolean Output  
6: Trigger Snapshot  
8: Center Storage  
9: Linkage Start Local Storage  
10: Linkage Stop Local Storage  
11: Trigger Snapshot Upload to FTP  
12: Trigger Snapshot Upload to E-mail  
13: Trigger Snapshot Upload to Both E-mail and FTP  
14: Smart Linkage Snapshot Upload  
15: Linkage Face Thumbnail Snapshot Upload  
16: Alarm Report  
19: Panoramic Linkage  
20: Attribute Collection  
21: Linkage Image Cloud Storage  
22: Auto Tracking  
28: Alarm Sound Arming  
29: Light Alarm Arming  
30: FTP Video Storage 
+++ActParam 
object 
body 
false

different linkage items; refer to [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter 
Table] for details.

Zhejiang Uniview Technologies Co., Ltd 
2025.5.16 
Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event 
6.10.12.9.6 Example for Response Parameters 
{ 
    "Response": { 
        "ResponseURL": "/LAPI/V1.0/Channels/<ID>/Smart/AccessZone/WeekPlan", 
        "CreatedID": -1, 
        "ResponseCode": 0, 
        "SubResponseCode": 0, 
        "ResponseString": "Succeed", 
        "StatusCode": 0, 
        "StatusString": "Succeed", 
        "Data": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "Num": 1, 
            "Days": [ 
                { 
                    "ID": 1, 
                    "Num": 1, 
                    "TimeSectionInfos": [ 
                        { 
                            "Begin": "00:00:00", 
                            "End": "23:59:59", 
                            "ArmingType": 0 
                        } 
                    ] 
                } 
            ] 
        } 
    } 
}

integer 
body 
true

Name 
Type 
Location 
Required 
Description 
+StatusString 
string 
body 
true 
Parsing results corresponding to status codes 
+Data 
object 
body 
true 
Response Result 
++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Plan Switch  
Leave blank to enable by default  
Default value: 1  
0: Disabled  
1: Enabled 
++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of Planned Days: Max. 7 (seven days a week) 
++Days 
object[] 
body 
true 
List of Daily Arming Plans for the Week 
+++ID 
integer 
body 
true 
Week Arming Index  
Default: 1  
0: Sunday  
1: Monday  
2: Tuesday  
3: Wednesday  
4: Thursday  
5: Friday  
6: Saturday 
+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments  
Value range: [1, 4] 
+++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

1: Monday

+++Num 
integer 
body 
true 
Number of time slots per day  
Max. Size: 4 segments

object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

The threshold value for the severe alarm rule must be more than 
the moderate alarm threshold. 
++Enabled 
integer 
body 
true 
0: Disable  
1: Enable 
++AlarmThermal 
integer 
body 
true 
Alarm Person Count Threshold 
ReportInterval 
integer 
body 
false 
Report interval time unit: (seconds) 
6.10.14.3.4 Example for Request Parameters 
{ 
    "GroupID": 0, 
    "GroupName": "CrowdDensityCnt", 
    "StatisticalType": 1, 
    "ReportInterval": 60, 
    "ChannelRuleNum": 0, 
    "ChannelRuleList": [], 
    "CrowdDensityRuleInfo": { 
        "MinorAlarmRuleInfo": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "AlarmThermal": 4 
        }, 
        "MajorAlarmRuleInfo": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "AlarmThermal": 10 
        }, 
        "CriticalAlarmRuleInfo": { 
            "Enabled": 1, 
            "AlarmThermal": 20 
        } 
    } 
}

++Num 
integer 
body

Number of Interlocks

object[] 
body

Interlock Action Information Table 
+++Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable  
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be included

Not supported by some devices  
0: Disabled  
1: Enabled

integer 
body 
true

object 
body

and ActParam can be found in [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table].

body

Actions 
object[] 
body 
false 
Interlock Action Information Table 
+Enabled 
integer 
body 
false 
Interlock Item Enable  
When ActID is greater than or equal to 28, it must be included  
(For compatibility, if less than 28, even if included, it will not be active)  
Not supported by some devices  
0: Disabled  
1: Enabled

integer 
body 
true

+ActParam 
object 
body 
true 
Linkage Action Parameter. The correspondence between specific ActID and 
ActParam can be found in [Alarm Linkage Action Parameter Table].

integer 
body

integer 
body 
true

object[] 
body 
true 
Arming Configuration Information  
On the same day, time periods must not overlap. 
++++Begin 
string 
body 
true 
Start Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++End 
string 
body 
true 
Stop Time 
Format hh:mm:ss 
String Length Range: [0, 11] 
++++ArmingType 
integer 
body 
true 
Arming Type  
All alarm arming types only support the schedule type, 
recording/snapshot plans are fully supported  
The default is schedule  
Some device models do not support this field  
0: Schedule  
1: Motion Detection  
2: Alarm  
3: Motion Detection and Alarm  
4: Motion Detection or Alarm  
5: No Plan  
10: Event

7.2.4.2 Parameter table 
Parameter names 
Type 
Required 
Description 
TimeMode 
integer 
true 
Linkage time Period Types 
0: User-defined schedule mode 
1: Day and night mode 
AudioCustomInfo 
object 
false 
Custom mode Linkage Sound Defense Information Details. This parameter is 
mandatory when the PUT message is set to 0 or the TimeMode message is 
set to 0 
+Num 
integer 
true 
Plan days 
IPC fixed at 7(7 days a week) 
+Days 
object[] 
true 
List of Monitor Plans for each day of the week 
++ID 
integer 
true 
Index of the Week 
1: Monday; 2: Tuesday; 
3: Wednesday; 4: Thursday; 
5: Friday; 6: Saturday; 
7: Sunday 
++Num 
integer 
true 
Number of time slots per day 
The number of IPC segments is fixed at 4 
++TimeSectionInfos 
object[] 
true 
Configuration Information about deploying defense in a time range 
Time segments of the same day cannot coincide 
+++Enabled 
integer 
true 
Enable the defense time range 
0: disables

Type

1: enabled 
+++Begin 
string 
true

string 
true

+++AudioActParamInfo 
object 
true 
Audible alarm configuration 
++++Enabled 
integer 
true 
Audible alarm linkage is enabled 
0: disables the alarm 
1: Enables the function 
++++AudioFileID 
integer 
true 
Audio file ID, used to identify different audio files 
Not supported by VMS 
++++WarnCount 
integer 
true 
Number of audible alarms, range: [1, 50], default 3 
Unit: Number of times 
Not supported by VMS 
++++AudioVolume 
integer 
false 
Volume size, used to adjust the output gain when the alarm is generated 
Range: [0, 255] 
Not supported by VMS 
++++VoiceTextContent 
string 
false 
For voice text content, the following properties can be used in addition to 
input text: 
Organization to which ${0} belongs 
The device that ${1} belongs to 
${2} Alarm source 
${3} Alarm time 
${4} Alarm type 
Supported by VMS only 
AudioDayNightInfo 
object 
false 
This parameter is mandatory when the PUT message and 
SupportDayNightMode is 1 and TimeMode is 1 or the GET message and 
SupportDayNightMode is 1

integer 
true 
Plan days 
IPC fixed at 7(seven days a week)

object[] 
true 
List of Monitor Plans for each day of the week

integer 
true 
Index of the Week 
1: Monday; 2: Tuesday; 
3: Wednesday; 4: Thursday; 
5: Friday; 6: Saturday; 
7: Sunday

object 
true 
Information about the defense configuration during the day 
+++Enabled 
integer 
true 
Enable defense deployment

1: enabled

object 
true

integer 
true

Type 
Required 
Description 
TimeMode 
integer 
true

LightCustomInfo 
object 
false

object 
true

integer 
false 
Light alarm linkage is enabled 
0: disables

integer 
true 
Light alarm interval, unit range: [5, 60], default 10s 
++Luminance 
integer 
false 
Light brightness, range: [1, 1000], default 50 
++LightTime 
integer 
false 
Light alarm interval, unit range: [5, 60], default 10s 
This field is used when the human figure is connected to the old light 
++Num 
integer 
false 
This parameter is mandatory when multiple types of lights are linked. The 
Actions parameter is used

object[] 
false 
List of linkage light type Information, optional when Num is 0

integer 
true
